children.text: New Leaf Hearing Care is an audiology and hearing aid clinic with trusted audiologists providing professional hearing services in Colorado.
We provide holistic hearing care to support lifelong wellness, cognitive health, meaningful connection, and active community engagement.
Visit us in Littleton, CO and Arvada, CO, to experience personalized, expert hearing care.
Why Entrust Your Hearing To New Leaf Hearing Care
Locally owned, independent audiology practice
You’re not a number here. Our care is built around relationships, trust, and long-term support.
Audiologists with personal experience with hearing loss
We understand the emotional side of hearing challenges and what it takes to navigate them with confidence.
Comprehensive care
Hearing healthcare should improve your daily life, not just your hearing test results. We look at the full picture: listening environments, communication needs, cognitive wellness, and comfort.
Education, counseling, and long-term support
We take time to explain your results, your options, and what to expect, so you can make informed decisions and feel supported at every step.
Community-focused with a commitment to giving back
We’re proud to serve Colorado families and invest in the communities where we live and work.
We listen first, educate clearly, and partner with you so you always know what’s happening, why it matters, and what comes next.
Hearing Care That Supports Your Whole Life

Hearing is closely connected to how you think, feel, and relate to the world around you. When hearing becomes difficult, people often start to withdraw from conversations, avoid social situations, or feel fatigued from trying to keep up.
Our approach is designed to help you:
Stay engaged with family, friends, and community
Reduce listening effort and mental fatigue
Improve confidence in noisy environments
Support cognitive and emotional wellness
Enjoy music, nature, and everyday moments again
Audiology Services in Colorado - Serving Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO
Hearing Assessments

New Leaf Hearing Care uses advanced testing and a detailed conversation about your lifestyle and listening challenges to identify the cause of hearing difficulties and recommend clear, personalized next steps.
Communication Training Program

At New Leaf Hearing Care, we take a holistic approach to hearing health through aural rehabilitation, combining hearing technology with education, coaching, and practical communication strategies to help you stay connected and confident.
LACE AI Pro

We offer LACE AI Pro as part of our holistic hearing rehabilitation approach. This app-based auditory training program helps strengthen your listening skills, especially in noisy environments, group conversations, and fast speech, so you can get real-world benefits from your hearing technology over time.
Tinnitus

At New Leaf Hearing Care, we provide personalized tinnitus counseling and management to help reduce the impact of ringing or buzzing in your ears. After a thorough evaluation, we’ll identify likely contributing factors and create a practical plan that may include education, sound therapy options, hearing technology support when appropriate, and strategies to improve comfort, sleep, and day-to-day focus.
Custom Ear Molds

Custom earmolds are made specifically for your ears to improve comfort, fit, and performance. At New Leaf Hearing Care, we create custom molds for hearing aids, hearing protection, and select assistive devices, helping reduce feedback, improve sound quality, and ensure a secure, comfortable fit for everyday use.
Ear Wax Removal

Excess earwax can cause muffled hearing, discomfort, itching, or even affect how well hearing aids work. At New Leaf Hearing Care, we offer safe, professional ear wax removal in Littleton, CO and Arvada, CO.
Hearing Aid Services
New Leaf Hearing Care’s hearing aid services are designed to help you hear comfortably and confidently in real life.
We guide you through hearing aid selection and styles, provide precise fittings, and perform expert programming and adjustments to match your hearing needs and lifestyle.
Using Real Ear Measurements for verification, we fine-tune performance for clarity and comfort, and we’re here long-term with reliable hearing aid repairs and ongoing support.
Hear Better in the Moments That Matter Most
Whether you’re noticing early changes in hearing, struggling in noise, or simply want a trusted team to guide your next steps, New Leaf Hearing Care is here to help.
Our audiology services are personalized, thorough, and focused on helping you stay connected to the life you love.
Call us today or schedule online to get started.

data.meta_description: Choosing a hearing aid feels easier with expert help. Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada or Littleton, CO today.

children.text: Choosing a hearing aid means matching the right device style and features to your hearing test results and your daily life.
The right match improves speech clarity, comfort, and confidence, so you keep up in real conversations.
New Leaf Hearing Care helps with choosing a hearing aid in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care).
Choosing a hearing aid can feel overwhelming at first. There are many brands, styles, and features to choose from.
New Leaf Hearing Care keeps the process clear and personal. We start with your hearing test, then we match solutions to your needs, your comfort, and your goals.
You can visit us in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO. The Littleton location is formerly Columbine Hearing Care and is now part of New Leaf Hearing Care.
Start With a Hearing Test You Can Trust
A hearing aid should never be picked from a chart or a website quiz. The right fit begins with accurate testing done by an audiologist.
Your hearing test shows what sounds you miss and what speech cues are hardest to catch. It also shows how each ear works on its own.
That information becomes your roadmap. It keeps the next step focused and prevents guesswork.
What You Want Your Hearing Aids to Do
A “good” hearing aid is not the same for everyone. The best choice is the one that supports your real life.
We ask questions like:
Which rooms feel hardest, and why?
Do you struggle most in noise or one-on-one?
Do you take phone calls for work or family?
Do you want rechargeable power?
Do you wear glasses, masks, or helmets often?
Those details shape the recommendation. This is hearing wellness, not a quick sale.
Hearing Aid Styles Explained in Plain Terms
Hearing aids come in a few main styles. Each style has strengths that fit certain ears and needs.
Behind-the-ear (BTE)
This style sits behind the ear with a piece that goes into the ear canal. It works well for many hearing levels and can feel stable during active days.
Receiver-in-canal (RIC)
This style sits behind the ear with a small wire to the ear canal. Many people like the comfort and the natural feel.
In-the-ear (ITE)
This style fits inside the outer ear. It can be easier to handle for some patients who want a single piece.
In-the-canal (ITC) and completely-in-canal (CIC)
These fit deeper in the ear canal and are less visible. Fit depends on ear shape, and handling can be harder for some hands.

Style choice depends on your hearing needs, your ear shape, and how you use your hands each day. Our audiologists at New Leaf Hearing Care will help you pick what works, not what looks good on a shelf.
Features That Make a Real Difference
Some features sound impressive, but do not help every patient. We focus on the ones that change daily listening.
Speech help in noise
Advanced microphones and noise controls help you follow speech in busy places. This matters for restaurants, events, and family gatherings.
Bluetooth phone and media
Many hearing aids can stream calls, music, and videos. This can make calls clearer and reduce the need to hold the phone in one “sweet spot.”
Rechargeable vs. disposable batteries
Rechargeable models reduce battery changes and can be simpler day to day. Disposable batteries work well for people who want quick swaps and longer storage.
Tinnitus support
Some devices include sound options that can help tinnitus feel less dominant. Your audiologist can pair this with counseling and a plan you can follow.
Keep in mind that hearing aid features should fit your life. A device packed with extras can still feel like the wrong choice if it does not match your needs.
Comfort and Fit Matter as Much as Sound
A hearing aid has to feel right, or it will sit in a drawer.
Comfort depends on shape, size, and how it seals in the ear.
Some patients do well with soft tips. Others need custom earmolds for a secure feel and steady sound.
Your provider checks how your hearing aids fit at every visit.
Why Programming and Verification Matter
Two people can wear the same model and have very different results. Programming is what makes a hearing aid match your hearing.
At New Leaf Hearing Care, we adjust settings based on your test and your feedback. We also use Real Ear Measurements, which check how the hearing aid performs in your ear.
This step matters because every ear shapes sound in a unique way. Verification confirms the device is doing what it should for you.
Your First Weeks Set the Tone
Your brain needs time to adjust to clearer sound. That is normal and expected.
We guide you through the early weeks with follow-up visits and fine-tuning. You learn how to wear the devices, how to care for them, and how to handle common listening challenges.
That support is part of the service, not an add-on.
Why Work With an Independent Audiology Clinic
Big box stores and online options push fast choices. A hearing aid is still medical-grade care, and it needs clinical support.
New Leaf Hearing Care focuses on people, not quotas. We build plans around health, connection, and long-term success.
If hearing changes later, your care plan can change too. You get a team, not a transaction.
Schedule a Hearing Consultation in Arvada or Littleton
Choosing hearing aids becomes simpler when someone takes time to listen. An audiologist can connect your hearing test, your lifestyle, and your comfort needs into one clear plan.
Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care). You will get guidance you can trust and support you can count on.
FAQs
What is choosing a hearing aid based on?
Choosing a hearing aid is based on your hearing test results, your ear shape, your comfort needs, and the places where you listen every day, like work, home, and restaurants.
How do I choose the right hearing aid style?
Your audiologist helps you choose the right style by checking your hearing level, looking at your ear canal size, and matching the device to how you use your hands, glasses, and masks.
How does a hearing aid fitting work?
A fitting includes selecting the right size, checking comfort, programming the hearing aids to match your hearing test, and teaching you how to use and clean them, followed by fine-tuning visits.
What is Real Ear Measurement and why does it matter?
Real Ear Measurement is an in-office check that measures how the hearing aid sounds in your ear, so the settings can be verified and adjusted for accurate speech clarity.
Ready to Choose With Confidence?
The right hearing aids help you stay connected to the people and moments that matter. Clear guidance also protects you from buying the wrong device and living with weak results.

Schedule a visit with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada or Littleton, CO. If you want an expert plan for choosing a hearing aid, start with an audiologist who can match your hearing, your life, and your goals.

data.meta_description: Build better daily conversations with our Communication Training Program. Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada or Littleton today.

children.text: A Communication Training Program teaches simple skills that help you follow speech, respond with confidence, and reduce mix-ups in daily talk.
Hearing devices help, but strong habits and clear strategies help you and your family communicate better in noise, groups, and busy places.
New Leaf Hearing Care offers a personalized Communication Training Program in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.

At New Leaf Hearing Care, we treat hearing as part of whole-life wellness. Better hearing supports brain health, close relationships, and an active life. A Communication Training Program helps you get more out of your hearing care.

Communication training is one of the most practical parts because it helps in real moments, not just in the clinic. It gives you tools and skills you can use right away at home, in the car, at dinner, and on the phone.
Our goal is simple. You leave with clear steps you can use in the places where you struggle most.
Why Communication Training Matters
Hearing loss changes how a conversation feels.
You work harder to keep up.
You miss small words that carry the meaning.
That strain can lead to fatigue and stress. It can also lead to pulling back from people you care about.
Communication training builds skills that lower that strain.
It helps you stay present and take part with more ease.
Part of a Holistic Hearing Plan
Audiologists call a full, skill-based plan “aural rehab.” That means care that supports your ears and your daily life.
Aural rehab can include hearing aids, assistive devices, counseling, and training
Communication training is one of the most practical parts because it helps in real moments, not just in the clinic.
Our Program: 5 Keys to Communication Success
New Leaf Hearing Care offers 5 Keys to Communication Success.
This program supports people with hearing loss and the people they talk with every day.
The program teaches clear communication strategies that are easy to use. The focus stays on daily life, not medical terms.
Many patients use this program alongside hearing aids. Others use it as a first step while they explore hearing solutions.
What You Learn in the 5 Keys Program
The program is built around five key parts of a strong conversation. Each part gives you a way to improve the outcome.
Environment
You learn how to set up the space so speech is easier to hear. Small changes can make a big difference.
Speaker
You learn how to guide family and friends to speak in a clear way. These tips keep the message clear without raising the volume.
Listener
You learn skills that help you catch speech and fill in missing pieces. You also learn how to ask for a repeat in a helpful way.
Practice
You build habits over time. Practice turns a strategy into a normal part of how you communicate.
Technology
You learn how to get the best use from hearing aids and other tools. This includes simple steps that support better sound and better comfort.
What You Receive in the 5 Keys Program
You get a structured plan that stays easy to follow.
Communication handbook with worksheets
You receive a printed handbook filled with tips and real-life examples. The worksheets help you build a plan for the situations that feel hardest.
Weekly lessons for one year
You receive short lessons once each week by email or through a mobile app. Each lesson includes guidance and extra resources you can use at your own pace.
Support from your audiology team
Your audiologist can help you apply the program to your life. You get help with goals, problem spots, and next steps.
Who Benefits from Communication Training
This program fits many people, including:
Adults who struggle to follow speech in noise.
People new to hearing aids who want faster progress.
Long-time hearing aid users who still feel stuck in groups.
Spouses and family members who want fewer mix-ups.
Anyone who wants less strain and more ease in daily talk.
The best results happen when a communication partner joins you. That shared effort lowers friction at home and builds teamwork.
What to Expect When You Get Started
Your first step is a visit with an audiologist. We learn where you struggle and what you want to change.
We may recommend a hearing test if you have not had one. Clear results help guide the right plan.
You then start the program with a simple path to follow. You will know what to do first, what to practice next, and how to measure progress.
Frequently Asked Questions
What is a Communication Training Program?
A Communication Training Program is a structured set of skills and strategies that helps you understand speech better and reduce stress in daily conversations.
How does the 5 Keys to Communication Success program work?
The 5 Keys program teaches five core parts of good communication and gives you a handbook plus weekly lessons for one year through email or a mobile app.
How do I get started with communication training at New Leaf Hearing Care?
You start by scheduling a consultation with an audiologist in Arvada or Littleton, then you receive the 5 Keys materials and a clear plan for how to use them.
What is the goal of communication training if I already have hearing aids?
The goal is to improve real-life conversation by adding skills and habits that make speech clearer in noise, groups, and fast talk, so hearing aids work better for your day.
Take the Next Step
Better hearing includes better communication. The right plan helps you stay connected to family, friends, and the life you enjoy.
Schedule your consultation with New Leaf Hearing Care and ask about our Communication Training Program in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO.

data.meta_description: Get custom earmolds in Arvada or Littleton. Improve comfort and performance for hearing aids or protection. Schedule your fitting today.

children.text: Custom earmolds are made from an impression of your ear, so the fit matches your ear shape instead of a one-size piece.
A secure seal improves comfort, reduces slipping, and boosts sound clarity or protection in the places you need it most.
New Leaf Hearing Care provides custom earmolds in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.
Custom earmolds solve problems that generic tips and plugs cannot fix. A poor fit can lead to discomfort, feedback, weak sound, or a plug that falls out at the worst time.
New Leaf Hearing Care creates custom earmolds to support hearing wellness and an active life. A proper fit improves comfort, supports stronger performance, and improves hearing.
Visit us in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO to get earmolds designed for your goals and your routine.
What Are Custom Earmolds?
Custom earmolds are made just for your ears. Your provider safely takes an impression of your ear canal, then a lab builds the mold to match that shape.
The final piece fits snugly and stays in place. You get a result that feels more natural than a generic option.
Many patients choose custom earmolds for hearing aids. Others need them for hearing protection, water protection, or communication devices.
Why Fit Matters So Much
Your ears are not shaped like anyone else’s. A “close enough” fit can create real issues.
A secure fit helps keep sound where it belongs. That improves clarity and reduces whistling for many hearing aid users.
Comfort matters too. An earmold that matches your ear reduces pressure points and rubbing that can make people stop wearing their devices.
A strong seal also helps hearing protection do its job. Noise reduction depends on the plug staying sealed during movement.
Common Uses for Custom Earmolds
Custom earmolds support different needs. Your provider helps you choose the style and features that match your day.
Hearing aids
Custom earmolds can improve sound delivery and keep devices stable. Many people choose them after struggling with domes that slip or feel loose.
Custom hearing protection
Workshops, job sites, and live music expose ears to harmful sound levels. Custom plugs offer reliable protection with a comfortable fit that you can wear longer.
Musician and performance use
In-ear monitors and music-focused molds help deliver clean sound and reduce outside noise. This supports safer listening habits during rehearsal and shows.
Water protection
Swim molds help keep water out of the ear canal. This is helpful for people with ear tubes, repeated ear infections, or sensitivity to water in the ears.
Communication devices
Radio and headset molds improve comfort and sound quality during long shifts. A better seal also reduces outside noise, so speech comes through more clearly.
Each option can be shaped, vented, and finished to fit your needs. Your audiologist guides the details so you get the right outcome.
Materials and Comfort Options
Custom earmolds come in different materials. Each choice supports a different goal.
Soft silicone
Silicone feels flexible and comfortable. Many patients choose it for comfort, strong sealing, and hearing protection.
Acrylic
Acrylic feels firmer and holds its shape well. Many labs use acrylic for styles that need more structure and easy cleaning.
Vinyl
Vinyl provides a balance of comfort and structure. It can be a good choice for everyday use when you want a durable, comfortable fit.
Color and style options can also be part of the build. Some people want a clear mold that blends in. Others prefer color for easy handling or quick identification.
What to Expect at Your Earmold Visit
The process is simple and guided from start to finish.
Your provider starts by looking in your ears. This step checks for wax or irritation that could affect the impression.
Next comes the impression. A soft material is placed in the ear to capture the shape of the ear canal.
The impression sets in a short time. Once removed, it becomes the blueprint used by the earmold lab.
After the lab completes your earmold, you return for fitting. Your provider checks comfort, seal, and function, then makes adjustments if needed.
Timing and Follow-Up
Custom earmolds take time to build because they are made for one person. A follow-up visit confirms fit and comfort.
This step is important for long-term success. A small change to a trim line or vent can improve comfort fast.
Your team also teaches you how to insert and remove the mold safely. That protects your ears and protects the device.
Care and Cleaning
Clean earmolds protect ear health and keep sound clear.
Daily wiping removes oils and debris. Regular, deeper cleaning helps prevent wax buildup in the sound path.
Your provider will show you the right method for your earmold type. That advice matters because cleaning tools differ based on material and design.
If you notice soreness, a change in sound, or a loose fit, schedule a check. A quick adjustment can prevent bigger problems.
Why Choose New Leaf Hearing Care
Custom earmolds require skill, accuracy, and follow-through. New Leaf Hearing Care provides all three.
We start with your real-life needs. Then we design an earmold that supports comfort, clarity, and long-term use.
Our team also stays with you after delivery. That support is the difference between “I tried it once” and “this works every day.”
FAQs
What is a custom earmold?
A custom earmold is a personalized earpiece made from an impression of your ear, designed to fit securely and comfortably for hearing aids, protection, or other in-ear devices.
How does the custom earmold process work?
Your provider checks your ears, takes an impression using a soft material, sends that impression to a lab, and then fits the finished earmold to confirm comfort and performance.
How do I know if I need custom earmolds for my hearing aids?
Custom earmolds are a strong choice when domes slip, feedback becomes a problem, or you need a tighter seal for better sound clarity and device stability.
Schedule Your Custom Earmold Visit
Comfort and fit shape your results every day. A better seal can improve clarity, reduce feedback, and make hearing protection feel easier to wear.
Schedule an appointment with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO.
Our team will guide you through the impression process and help you choose the right style for your needs.
If you want a secure, comfortable fit that supports real life, start with custom earmolds today.

data.meta_description: Get safe ear wax removal in Arvada or Littleton. Clear blockage and improve comfort. Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care today.

children.text: Ear wax removal is an in-office service that clears extra wax from the ear canal to restore comfort and clearer sound.
Built-up wax can cause muffled hearing, ringing, pressure, and poor hearing aid performance, and professional removal protects the ear.
New Leaf Hearing Care provides ear wax removal in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.
Ear wax removal is a simple service that can make a big change fast. When wax builds up, sound gets blocked, and the ear can feel full or sore.
New Leaf Hearing Care offers safe, professional ear wax removal Colorado. The Littleton office is formerly known as Columbine Hearing Care and is now part of New Leaf Hearing Care.
Why Ear Wax Buildup Happens
Ear wax is normal. It helps protect the ear canal from dust and germs.
Problems start when wax does not move out on its own. A narrow ear canal, dry wax, hearing aids, earbuds, and even cotton swabs can push wax deeper.
Once wax is packed in, hearing can drop quickly. Many people notice it in one ear first.
Signs You May Need Ear Wax Removal
Wax buildup can feel like more than one issue at the same time.
Below are signs that you might need ear wax removal:
muffled hearing
pressure or a “plugged” feeling in the ears
itching in the ears
ringing in the ears
hearing aids may whistle or sound weak
If any of these are happening, a visit to an audiologist can give you clear answers and fast help.
Why Professional Ear Wax Removal Is the Safer Choice

Professional ear wax removal is the safer choice because an audiologist can see the wax clearly, choose the right method for your ear, and remove any blockage without damaging the delicate skin of the ear canal or pushing it deeper.
At-home tools like cotton swabs and picks may pose a risk of pushing the wax deeper in the ears, which can make hearing feel more muffled and increase pressure or pain.
Ear candling adds risk of burns and still does not clear wax from the ear canal.
In the clinic, an audiologist or hearing care professional removes wax in a controlled, careful way, helping you get faster relief and protecting your ear health at the same time.
What Happens at Your Appointment
Your visit starts with a close look inside your ears. This confirms wax buildup and rules out other causes of symptoms.
Next, your provider chooses the safest removal method for your ear and the type of wax present. Our clinic uses methods such as irrigation or curettage, based on what your ear needs.
Many visits also include tympanometry to confirm healthy middle ear function.
After removal, your provider checks the canal again. You leave knowing what was removed, how your ears look, and what to do next.
How Long Does Ear Wax Removal Take?
Most earwax removal visits fit into a short appointment window. The time depends on the amount of wax and how firm it is.
Some blockages clear in minutes. Impacted wax can take longer because care must stay gentle and precise.
Your comfort remains the priority during the entire visit.
Ear Wax Removal and Hearing Aids
Earwax and hearing aids affect each other.
Wax can block sound from reaching the eardrum. It can make hearing aids seem weak, even when the devices are working.
Wax can also clog hearing aid receivers and earmolds. This leads to poor sound and more repairs.
A clean ear canal helps your hearing aids perform the way they should. It also helps your audiologist adjust settings with more accuracy.
Prevention Tips That Protect Your Ears
A few habits reduce repeat buildup, such as:
Skip cotton swabs inside the ear canal. They push wax deeper and irritate the skin.
Use hearing aid wax guards.
Follow the cleaning method and frequency your provider recommends.
Ask your audiologist how often you should schedule ear checks. People who wear hearing aids or earbuds daily benefit from routine monitoring.
If you suspect an earwax blockage again, see an audiologist. Early wax removal is easier and more comfortable.
Why Choose New Leaf Hearing Care
Earwax problems feel small until they disrupt daily life. A blocked ear can affect work calls, family conversations, and balance.
Our team takes a calm, careful approach. You get clear communication and an office that respects your time.
You also get follow-up guidance. If wax buildup ties into hearing aid care or hearing changes, your audiologist can address the full picture.

That full-service support is part of hearing wellness offered at New Leaf Hearing Care. We have two locations conveniently serving the community in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care).
FAQs
What is ear wax removal?
Ear wax removal is a clinical service that clears excess wax from the ear canal using safe tools and methods, so sound can travel normally and the ear feels comfortable again.
How does ear wax removal work at New Leaf Hearing Care?
Your provider looks in your ears, selects the right method for your wax type, removes the blockage, and checks your ear canal again to confirm the wax is cleared.
How do I know if my hearing problem is wax or hearing loss?
A blocked ear can cause sudden muffled hearing, pressure, and ringing, while hearing loss tends to build over time. An in-office ear check and hearing test confirm the cause.
Schedule Your Ear Wax Removal Today
A blocked ear can make everything feel harder, from conversations to simple daily sounds. Professional care can clear the problem fast and protect your ear health at the same time.
Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care).
If you want relief and clear guidance, start with safe, professional ear wax removal today.

data.meta_description: Get quick answers about hearing tests, hearing aids, and our care approach. Contact New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada or Littleton today.

children.text: If you have questions about hearing tests, hearing aids, or what to expect from care, you’re not alone.
Below are clear answers to common questions we hear from our patients in Arvada and Littleton, CO.
Q: Do I really need a hearing test if I feel like I hear okay?
A: Many hearing changes happen gradually. A baseline hearing test helps identify early changes and protect long-term hearing and brain health.
Q: Are hearing aids only for older adults?
A: No. Hearing loss affects people of all ages, and early treatment leads to better outcomes.
Q: How are you different from big box hearing aid stores?
A: We provide medical-level care, personalized treatment, and long-term support — not just device sales.
Q: Will hearing aids help my brain health?
A: Yes. Research shows that treating hearing loss supports cognitive health and reduces listening fatigue.
If you still have questions, our team is here to help.
Schedule a visit and get answers that fit your hearing needs, lifestyle, and goals.

data.meta_title: Giving Back | Helping Our Community Hear and Thrive | New Leaf Hearing Care
data.meta_description: Support accessible hearing care locally and worldwide through our Hearing the Call partnership. Learn how we give back. Contact us today.

children.text: At New Leaf Hearing Care, we believe that hearing care is a bridge to connection, and that bridge should be accessible to everyone.
Our passion for service extends beyond our clinic walls - from the local neighborhoods of Arvada and the Denver Metro area to global humanitarian missions.
When you choose New Leaf Hearing Care, you aren't just improving your own life; you are helping us provide the gift of sound to those in need.
Our Partnership With Hearing the Call
We are proud to partner with Hearing the Call, a non-profit organization focused on making professional hearing healthcare accessible to individuals locally and globally.
Global Missions
Our team, including Dr. Jessen, has participated in humanitarian clinics in Guatemala, Jamaica, Brazil, and the Turks & Caicos Islands.
Local Impact
Through Hearing the Call – Colorado, we provide essential services to neighbors in our community who otherwise could not afford treatment.
How You Can Help
Choosing New Leaf Hearing Care does more than improve your hearing - it helps your community hear, too.
New Leaf Shares
Through New Leaf Shares, your care fuels real support: we donate $10 for every hearing aid purchased and $100 for every friend or family referral who begins treatment.
One decision, wider impact - helping make hearing care more accessible across the Arvada and Littleton, CO communities.
Used Hearing Aid Donations
Have an old device gathering dust? We welcome donations of used hearing aids. These can be dropped off at our clinic during normal working hours to be refurbished for those in need.
Monetary Contributions
Financial gifts help cover the "hidden" costs of care, such as earmolds, medical supplies, and transportation for patients to attend follow-up appointments
More Ways We Give Back
Our leadership’s heart for service extends to many incredible organizations. When you support New Leaf Hearing Care, you help us continue our financial and volunteer support for these vital causes:
St. Jude Children's Research Hospital

We have supported St. Jude for decades. Our contributions help ensure that families dealing with childhood cancer can focus on their child’s health without ever receiving a bill for treatment, travel, or housing.
Heifer International

By partnering with communities worldwide, Heifer International helps end hunger and poverty. They provide livestock and training to families, allowing them to build sustainable businesses and lift up the next generation of leaders.
Freedom Service Dogs

This organization rescues shelter dogs and transforms them into life-changing service dogs for veterans and first responders. Our team has a personal history of volunteering and even adopting "career change" pups like Golly, who was a beloved family member for 9 years.
Friends of Man

Dr. Dusty serves as a professional advisor for this 100% volunteer-run charity. She reviews hearing aid requests to ensure applicants receive the proper medical devices and mobility equipment they need to regain their independence.
Cycling Without Age

We are proud to support the Littleton chapter of this national non-profit. Whether it’s providing "trishaw" bike rides to seniors or assisting with graphic design for their outreach, we love helping our neighbors feel the "wind in their hair."

children.text: Better Hearing for You. The Gift of Hearing for Someone in Need.
Hear It Forward is a membership built for people who want great hearing care without feeling trapped by big, upfront costs.
You get premium hearing technology, expert care, and long-term support under a simple monthly plan.
Your membership also helps fund hearing care for someone who would not have access otherwise through Hearing the Call – Colorado.
Why This Membership Exists
Better hearing changes daily life.
It supports confidence, connection, and clear communication with the people you love.
Hear It Forward was created to make the path to better hearing easier to start, easier to maintain, and meaningful beyond the clinic.
What’s Included in Membership
This membership is designed to cover the parts of hearing care that matter most in real life.
You can expect:
Premium hearing technology selected and fitted for your needs
Professional programming and fine-tuning for comfort and clarity
Routine cleanings, maintenance, and support
Repairs and troubleshooting when things go wrong
Loaner support when your devices need service
Ongoing follow-ups to keep your hearing on track
Planned upgrades on a set schedule, so your technology stays current
How Hear It Forward Works
Step 1: Start With a Hearing Wellness Screening
We begin with a clear look at your hearing and daily listening needs.
We learn where speech breaks down for you, how much effort you spend listening, and what outcomes matter most.
Step 2: Comprehensive Hearing Evaluation
If treatment is recommended, your next visit includes complete diagnostic testing and a care plan built around your lifestyle.
You get clear answers and a path forward.
Step 3: Join the Membership
You will be professionally fitted with premium hearing technology, fully activated and onboarded for a simple, flat $999 activation fee.
Your professional fitting will include Real Ear Measurements, the gold standard in hearing aid fitting and programming.
Step 4: Ongoing Support That Actually Lasts
Hearing care is not “one and done.”  Regular follow-up visits and professional care are critical to the success of hearing loss treatment.
Your monthly $127 Hear it Forward Membership fee covers all follow-up visits and support so your devices keep performing optimally in settings like restaurants, meetings, and family gatherings.
Step 5: Giving Back With Every Membership

Your membership helps fund hearing care for someone in need through Hearing the Call – Colorado.  Every three years, you will be fitted with the newest generation of premium hearing devices.  Your previous devices will be reconditioned and donated to a person in need through Hearing the Call - Colorado.
Your improved hearing becomes a gift that reaches beyond your home.
Step 6: Seamless Upgrades on a Set Schedule
Hearing technology changes fast, but most people wait 5-7 years, or more, to upgrade their hearing aids due to the high up-front costs.
Your Hear it Forward Membership includes technology upgrades every 3 years, so you are always hearing with the latest and best technology available.
The Impact: Your Hearing Creates Someone Else’s First Clear Conversation
When someone can hear clearly again, they reconnect with:
Family conversations
Work and daily independence
Safety cues and awareness
Community and relationships
Hear It Forward turns hearing care into a cycle of support.
Your membership helps another person experience that moment when speech becomes clear and connection is easy again.
Who Hear It Forward Is For
This membership is a strong fit for people who want:
Predictable monthly costs
Premium technology and professional care
Ongoing support
A plan that fits long-term needs
A meaningful way to give back through hearing care
If you want care that supports a full, active life, this membership makes it easier to start and easier to stay consistent.
Hear It Forward Membership FAQs
What is the Hear It Forward Membership?
Hear It Forward is a hearing care membership that bundles premium hearing technology, professional care, support, and technology upgrades every 3 years.
What is the cost of the Hear It Forward Membership?
There is a one-time activation fee of $999 and a monthly membership fee of $127, beginning on the day of your hearing aid fitting.
What if I don’t want to continue my membership after 3 years?
Your membership contract requires 36 monthly payments of $127.  You may cancel after the 36th payment.  At that time, you are required to return the hearing devices to our clinic.
How does Hear It Forward help someone else?
Each membership helps fund hearing care for people who cannot afford it through Hearing the Call – Colorado, extending the impact beyond your own care.
How do I get started?
Schedule a consultation at New Leaf Hearing Care. We’ll review your hearing needs, explain the membership details, and guide your next step.
Ready to Hear Better and Help Someone Else Hear?
If you want premium care, predictable costs, and a purpose behind your investment, Hear It Forward is built for you.
Contact New Leaf Hearing Care to learn more about this wonderful opportunity to give back!

data.meta_description: Upgrade your hearing with hearing aid accessories in Arvada or Littleton. Ask New Leaf Hearing Care what fits your needs. Schedule today.

children.text: Hearing aid accessories are add-ons and care tools that improve how your hearing aids sound, connect, and hold up day to day.
The right hearing aid accessory helps you hear speech more clearly in tough places, stay connected to devices, and keep your hearing aids working well.
New Leaf Hearing Care offers personalized hearing aid accessories in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.

Hearing aid accessories help fix the real-world problems that can show up after a fitting. Even a well-programmed hearing aid can struggle in a loud restaurant, a meeting room, or during a car ride, where noise and distance make speech harder to follow.
New Leaf Hearing Care helps you choose accessories that match your hearing goals and your routine. The goal stays simple: clearer speech, easier use, and fewer daily hassles.

You can schedule an appointment with our trusted audiologists in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO. The Littleton location is formerly known as Columbine Hearing Care and is now part of New Leaf Hearing Care.
Why Hearing Aid Accessories Matter in Real Life
Life is not a quiet test booth. Sound comes from every direction, and distance changes what you can catch.
Hearing aid accessories help close that gap. They bring speech closer, cut down competing noise, and make sound more direct.
When used correctly, hearing aid accessories also make hearing aids easier to use. That means more wear time, less frustration, and better results.
The Main Types of Hearing Aid Accessories
Hearing aid accessories generally fit into two categories.
Performance Accessories (Hear Better in Real Life)
These help you hear speech more clearly across distance, in noise, and in group settings -  examples include remote microphones, TV streamers, and other listening tools.
Care & Protection Accessories (Keep Devices Working Well)
These protect your investment and reduce breakdowns - examples include cleaning tools, wax guards, dryers/dehumidifiers, extra chargers, and retention options.
An audiologist will recommend the right combination based on where you want the biggest day-to-day improvement first.
Assistive Listening Devices for Distance and Noise
When a speaker is far away, speech drops fast. Noise and poor room acoustics make it harder.
Assistive listening devices send the sound to you in a clean way that can come through your hearing aids or through a receiver worn near the ear.
Many public places also offer built-in support, like audio loop systems. Your audiologist can help you learn what your hearing aids can connect to and how to use it.
Wireless Streaming for TV, Phone, and More
Wireless accessories connect your hearing aids to everyday tech including TVs, phones, tablets, and computers.
Streaming helps because sound goes right to your hearing aids. You hear the signal clearly without turning the volume up for everyone else.
Phone calls can also feel easier with streaming. Speech becomes more direct, and the phone no longer has to sit in a perfect position near the microphone.
Remote Microphones for Clearer One-on-One Speech
A remote mic helps when one voice matters most. It clips to a partner’s shirt, sits on a table, or can be placed near a speaker.
The mic sends the voice straight to your hearing aids which reduces the impact of distance and room noise.
Many patients use a remote mic for restaurants, meetings, church, fitness classes, and car rides. One small tool can change how the entire moment feels.
Helpful Tools for Daily Hearing Aid Care
Sound quality depends on clean parts and dry electronics. Care tools keep small problems from turning into repairs.
The following accessories support daily success:
Cleaning tools and kits
Brushes, picks, and wipes help remove wax and debris. Regular cleaning protects microphones and speakers.
Wax guards and filters
Wax guards/filters protect key parts from wax buildup. Fresh guards keep sound clear and prevent blockages that cause weak volume.
Dryers and dehumidifiers
Moisture breaks down electronics and shortens device life. A dryer pulls out moisture overnight and supports stable performance.
Extra chargers and travel power
A second charger supports travel and busy schedules. A portable charging case helps people who spend long hours away from an outlet.
Retention clips and sport locks
Loss prevention matters, especially for active days. Retention options keep hearing aids secure during exercise and outdoor time.
Our team at New Leaf Hearing Care will help show you which supplies match your device model. This prevents wasted purchases and prevents damage from the wrong part.
Hearing Aid Accessories Many Patients Ask About
Some accessories stand out because they solve common pain points.
Portable microphones for changing settings
Products like table mics and partner mics are built for speech in noise and group talk. They work well in places where hearing aids alone feel strained.
TV connectors
A TV connector streams sound straight to your hearing aids. Many families like this option because you can keep the room volume comfortable for everyone.
An audiologist can walk you through what fits your hearing aids and what pairing looks like at home.
How New Leaf Hearing Care Can Help
A smart accessory plan starts with a short consultation. We learn where you struggle, what devices you use each day, and what a “win” looks like for you.
Then we recommend the accessories that match those needs. You get a clear reason for every recommendation.
Training matters too. We help with setup, pairing, and real-world tips, so the accessory becomes part of your life instead of sitting in a drawer.
Common Problems Hearing Aid Accessories Solve
Accessories are not just “extras.” They are solutions for daily challenges, including:
TV that feels too loud for others, yet still unclear to you.
Group dinners where speech blends together.
Meetings where one speaker is too far away.
Car rides where road noise covers words.
Sweat or humidity that affects device sound.
Wax buildup that makes devices seem weak.
When the right tool is added, hearing improves and conversation becomes easier to follow.
A Wellness Approach to Better Hearing
New Leaf Hearing Care focuses on hearing wellness for a full, active life. Accessories support that goal because they make it easier to stay engaged.
Clearer sound supports better connection. Lower listening effort supports better energy and focus.
That is why we treat accessories as part of the plan, not an afterthought.
Visit Us in Arvada or Littleton
Both our clinics provide the same level of support.
Arvada, CO
Accessory guidance, pairing support, and ongoing hearing aid care.
Littleton, CO
Formerly Columbine Hearing Care, now part of New Leaf Hearing Care, with the same patient-first approach and long-term support.
Bring your hearing aids and your questions. We will help you choose tools that fit your needs and budget.
FAQs
What are hearing aid accessories?
Hearing aid accessories are add-on devices and care supplies that improve hearing aid performance, help you connect to phones and TVs, and support daily cleaning and moisture control.
How do hearing aid accessories help in noisy places?
Accessories like remote microphones and assistive listening devices send speech directly to your hearing aids, which improves clarity when background noise and distance make listening hard.
How do I know which hearing aid accessories I need?
A consultation with an audiologist connects your hearing test results to your daily listening problems, then matches you with accessories that solve the specific situations you want to improve.
How does a TV accessory work with hearing aids?
A TV streaming accessory sends the TV sound directly to your hearing aids, so speech is clearer and the room volume can stay comfortable for others.
Schedule a Consultation Today

If your hearing aids work well in quiet rooms but fall short in daily life, accessories can close the gap fast. One focused visit can lead to clearer TV sound, stronger speech understanding in noise, and easier device care at home.
Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO. Our team will guide you to the right hearing aid accessories and show you how to use them with confidence.

children.text: Hearing aid fittings are appointments where an audiologist programs your devices, checks comfort, and sets them up for your real-life listening needs.
A precise hearing aid fitting improves speech clarity, reduces feedback, and helps you feel confident wearing your hearing aids every day.
New Leaf Hearing Care provides professional hearing aid fittings in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care).
No two ears are the same, so no fitting should be generic. A proper hearing aid fitting customizes sound and comfort to your hearing needs, your ear shape, and your daily routine.
New Leaf Hearing Care offers hearing aid fittings in Arvada and Littleton, CO. The Littleton office is formerly known as Columbine Hearing Care and is now part of New Leaf Hearing Care.
Our goal is simple. Help you hear better and live more fully, with care that supports your brain health, relationships, and daily confidence.
What a Hearing Aid Fitting Includes
A fitting is more than putting hearing aids in your ears. This visit turns your hearing test results into settings built for you.
Your audiologist will:
Confirm the physical fit.
Program the hearing aids to match your hearing needs.
Set comfort levels so sound feels natural.
Teach you how to use and care for the devices.
Make sure you feel ready to wear them in daily life.
You leave with a clear plan for what happens next and how we support you over time.
Why Fit and Comfort Matter So Much
If hearing aids pinch, slip, or feel awkward, most people stop wearing them. This slows progress and makes adjustments harder.
A secure fit helps sound reach your ear the right way. Comfort helps you wear the devices long enough for your brain to adjust.
Daily wear builds results. That is why we take hearing aid fittings seriously.
Programming That Matches Your Hearing
Hearing aids are computers for your ears. They need the right settings to support speech and reduce strain.
During your fitting, your audiologist adjusts sound levels for different pitches. That helps speech come through clearly without feeling too sharp.
We also set features that support real-world listening including noise reduction and direction settings that help in busy places.
The goal is balance: clear speech without harsh sound.
Real Ear Measurements and Verification
Every ear shapes sound in a unique way. A setting that looks right on a screen can sound different once it is in your ear.
Real Ear Measurements check how sound performs in your ear canal. This helps confirm your hearing aids are delivering the right sound where it matters.
Verification supports better results and fewer frustrations. It also helps keep volume at a safe and comfortable level.
Learning Your New Hearing Aids
Even the best fitting needs good coaching. Small skills make a big difference in daily success.
We teach you how to:
Insert and remove your hearing aids with a secure, comfortable fit.
Keep power reliable with charging steps or battery changes that match your device.
Use volume and listening programs the right way for different settings.
Clean your devices safely to protect sound quality and prevent damage.
Catch wax buildup early so small issues don’t turn into poor performance.
You also learn what is normal in the first days. Some sounds will feel new again, like footsteps, paper, and running water.
Your brain needs time to re-learn these sounds. Consistent wear helps that process move faster.
Your First Weeks: What Progress Looks Like
Most patients notice improvement right away in quiet settings. Busy places take more time.
Restaurants, group talks, and meetings are more complex. Your brain has to sort speech from background noise.
That is why follow-up care matters. We fine-tune based on your real experiences, not guesses.
A good fit starts the process. Ongoing support completes it.
Follow-Up Visits and Fine-Tuning
Hearing aid care is not one appointment. Small changes improve comfort and clarity over time.
Follow-up visits allow us to:
Reduce feedback and whistling.
Adjust sound that feels too sharp or too dull.
Improve clarity in noisy places.
Update settings after you share real-life examples.
These visits also build confidence. You learn what your hearing aids can do and how to use them well.
Custom Earmolds When You Need a Stronger Fit
Some patients do well with standard ear tips while others need a more secure solution.
Custom earmolds are made from an impression of your ear and can improve comfort, stability, and sound delivery.
Earmolds also help reduce feedback for some hearing aid users. An audiologist will guide you if this option fits your needs.
Hearing Aid Fittings as Part of Hearing Wellness
At New Leaf Hearing Care, hearing care supports whole-life wellness. Better hearing helps you stay connected and engaged.
A proper fitting supports:
Clearer conversations at home.
More confidence in public places.
Less listening effort at the end of the day.
More comfort during work calls and social time.
Healthy hearing supports healthy living. A fitting is the step that turns technology into real benefits.
Why Work With an Audiologist for Your Hearing Aid Fitting
Online sellers and big-box stores move fast. Hearing care works best when it is personal and precise.
An audiologist uses your hearing test, your lifestyle, and your comfort needs to guide every setting. That approach helps you avoid common issues like underpowered sound, poor fit, and low wear time.
You also get a long-term partner. If your hearing changes, your plan can change too.
FAQs
What is a hearing aid fitting?
A hearing aid fitting is an appointment where an audiologist programs your hearing aids to match your hearing test, checks comfort and fit, and teaches you how to use and care for your devices.
How does a hearing aid fitting work at New Leaf Hearing Care?
Your audiologist confirms the fit, programs settings based on your hearing needs, verifies sound performance when appropriate, and gives you hands-on training for daily use and cleaning.
How do I know if my hearing aids are fitted correctly?
A correct fit feels secure and comfortable, speech sounds clearer, and you can wear the devices for long periods without soreness, whistling, or frequent slipping.
How does follow-up care improve hearing aid results?
Follow-up visits let your audiologist fine-tune settings using your real-life feedback, which improves clarity in noise, reduces listening effort, and increases comfort over time.
Schedule Your Hearing Aid Fitting in Colorado
A good fit can change how your hearing aids feel and how conversations sound. The right setup supports comfort, clarity, and confidence in daily life.
Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO. If you want a hearing management plan that helps you succeed long term, start with professional hearing aid fittings today.

data.meta_title: Hearing Aid Funding Resources | New Leaf Hearing Care
data.meta_description: Find hearing aid funding resources in Colorado. Explore nonprofit and state options that may help cover costs. Contact New Leaf Hearing Care today.

children.text: At New Leaf Hearing Care, we know that paying for hearing aids can be a heavy financial burden for those with limited or fixed incomes. We are proud to offer affordable services and a range of hearing aid technologies to fit most budgets. We are also happy to offer no-interest monthly payment options. Please see our
Paying for Your Visit
page for more details.
However, if the costs of hearing loss treatment are still outside of your budget, we encourage you to look into the following funding options available to adults in Colorado.
Hearing the Call – Colorado

New Leaf Hearing Care is a proud partner of this non-profit organization that provides hearing aids to income-qualified applicants. Hearing aids and 3 follow-up visits are fully paid for by Hearing the Call. Appointments beyond the first 3 are just $15/visit.

Our non-profit works on the principle of the Circle of Giving. We only ask our hearing aid recipients to “pay it forward” through 10 hours of service or 10 acts of kindness in their community. For more information and to apply, go to HearingTheCall.org/Colorado.
Friends of Man

Friends of Man is a 501(c)(3), non-profit, tax-exempt charity that uses 100% of donations for charitable assistance. Friends of man accepts applications for many kinds of medical devices, including hearing aids. To learn more and apply, go to FriendsOfMan.org.
Colorado Division of Vocational Rehabilitation (DVR)

This is a state and federally-funded program designed to help eligible individuals with disabilities find and maintain suitable employment, which includes assistance with procuring hearing aids.  You must have a job or be actively looking for a job to be a DVR client. For more information and to apply, go to DVR.Colorado.Gov.
Colorado Gerontological Society (CGS)

Through a grant from the Denver Regional Council of Government and community donations, CGS provides financial assistance to Denver metro area seniors for the purchase of eyeglasses and hearing aids. Applicants must be adults aged 60 and over who live in Adams, Arapahoe, Broomfield, Clear Creek, Denver, Douglas, Gilpin, or Jefferson County. For more information and to apply, go to SeniorAnswers.org.
Denver Health Audiology Clinic

Hearing aids can be purchased at a reduced cost if you have Medicare (non-HMO) and/or Medicaid. You must be a Colorado resident and live in the Denver Metro Area. A referral from your primary care physician is required before an appointment can be scheduled. For more information, go to DenverHealth.org/Services/Audiology.
Denver Lions Club

The Hearing Committee of the Denver Lions Club provides funding for hearing aids and fitting fees to income-qualified individuals living in the Denver Metro area. For more information, go to DenverLions.org.
Marion Downs Center
This non-profit organization offers financial assistance to qualified applicants for hearing tests and hearing aids. For more information, go to MarionDowns.org.

children.text: Hearing aid repairs fix problems like weak sound, whistling, broken parts, or devices that will not turn on or charge.
Quick, professional service restores clear hearing sooner and helps protect your devices from bigger damage.
New Leaf Hearing Care provides trusted hearing aid repairs in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.
When a hearing aid stops working, daily life gets harder fast. Phone calls feel stressful. Conversations feel strained. Even quiet moments can feel off.
New Leaf Hearing Care offers reliable hearing aid repairs in Arvada and Littleton, CO, so you can get back to clearer hearing with less delay.
Our team will troubleshoot the problem, clean and service your devices, and guide you on the next best step.
Common Signs Your Hearing Aid Needs Repair
Hearing aids rarely fail all at once. Small issues show up first.
Sound may fade or cut in and out.
Volume may feel weaker than normal.
A whistle may start when you talk or chew.
The device may feel loose, even when it is in the right spot.
Batteries may drain too fast or chargers may not connect well.
These problems have real causes.
A repair visit helps you find the reason and fix it the right way.
What Causes Hearing Aid Problems
Most hearing aid issues come from a few sources.
Wax and debris block sound. Moisture affects small electronics. Drops and impacts crack shells or break parts.
A damaged receiver, microphone, or battery contact can also cause poor sound. Sometimes the device is fine, but a setting needs adjustment after a hearing change.
A trained team can tell the difference which prevents wasted time and money.
New Leaf Hearing Care offers hearing aid repairs and maintenance in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO (formerly known as Columbine Hearing Care).
What We Can Fix in the Office
Many issues can be corrected during a clinic visit.
Professional cleaning often restores sound right away. Wax filters, domes, tubing, and battery contacts can be replaced when worn out.
We can also check the fit and reduce feedback. Programming changes can improve clarity if your hearing needs have shifted.
If the device needs deeper repair, we will explain what must be sent out and what to expect.
When a Repair Needs to Be Sent Out
Some problems require lab service. Internal components can fail. Shells can crack. Charging systems can stop working.
When a repair needs to leave the clinic, we help you handle it with less stress. Our team documents the issue, prepares the device, and manages the process so you are not left guessing.
You will also get clear guidance on what to do while you wait, including options for staying connected day to day.
A Repair Visit Is Also a Performance Check
Many people come in for repairs and leave with more than a fix. A service visit gives your hearing aids a full check.
We review how the devices sound and feel. We confirm the parts fit properly and stay secure.
Small updates can make a big change, especially when your hearing aids have been through seasons of sweat, humidity, and daily wear.
Simple Steps to Try Before Your Appointment
A few quick checks can save time and get you back on track sooner.
Start by replacing the battery or charging fully. Check the wax filter and replace it if it looks blocked.
Confirm the ear tip or mold is seated correctly. A loose fit can cause feedback and weak sound.
If sound still seems off, bring the devices in. A professional check protects your ears and your devices.
Our Littleton, CO and Arvada, CO audiologists are trained to check and perform minor repairs for hearing aids.
Maintenance That Prevents Repeat Repairs
Repairs become less common when care becomes routine. A few habits keep devices working well:
Wipe hearing aids daily with a dry cloth.
Brush off wax and debris using the tools provided.
Keep devices dry overnight, especially after sweat or bad weather.
Store hearing aids away from bathroom steam and direct heat.
Replace wax guards and tips on schedule.
Clean parts support clear sound and reduce strain on the electronics.
Our audiology team at New Leaf Hearing Care can show you the right method for your device style.
How Hearing Aid Repairs Support Hearing Wellness
Hearing health is not only about buying devices. Long-term success depends on devices working well and feeling comfortable.
A working hearing aid supports better focus, better connection, and less listening effort.
New Leaf Hearing Care follows a hearing wellness approach, where repairs support more than the device - they support your daily life.
Keeping your hearing aids working as they should helps you stay comfortable in conversations, confident in public, and fully engaged in the activities and people that matter most.
Why Choose New Leaf Hearing Care
Hearing aid repairs work best when the provider knows your devices and your goals. You want a team that listens and takes time to solve the root issue.
Our clinic provides practical support without pressure. We explain what is wrong, what can be fixed now, and what needs more work.
Long-term care matters too. We help you avoid repeat problems by keeping your devices clean, tuned, and performing the way they should.
FAQs
What are hearing aid repairs?
Hearing aid repairs are services that fix device problems such as weak sound, feedback, broken parts, moisture damage, charging issues, and devices that will not power on.
How do I know if my hearing aid needs repair or a cleaning?

A blocked wax filter or dirty microphone can cause muffled sound and cutting out, while broken parts and charging problems point to repair needs. A clinic visit confirms the cause and fixes it safely.
How does a hearing aid repair appointment work?
A repair visit includes device inspection, troubleshooting, professional cleaning, part replacement, when needed, and programming adjustments when sound settings need to be updated.
How do I prevent hearing aid repairs in the future?
Daily wiping, routine filter changes, safe storage, and moisture control reduce common failures and keep the sound clear and steady over time.
Schedule Your Hearing Aid Repair At New Leaf Hearing Care

A hearing aid problem can interrupt your life in one day. Quick service gets your hearing aids back to comfortable, reliable use, so conversations feel easier and you stay present for the people and moments that matter most.
Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO.
Our team will troubleshoot the issue, explain your options, and get you back on track with dependable hearing aid repairs.

Picking between hearing aid styles is one of the biggest steps in hearing care. The style affects comfort, how easy it is to handle, and how well you hear in real life.
New Leaf Hearing Care helps you choose a style that fits your hearing needs and your day-to-day routine. You get guidance from an audiologist, not a sales pitch.
Visit us in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO. The Littleton location is formerly Columbine Hearing Care and is now part of New Leaf Hearing Care.
A Quick Tip Before You Compare Styles
A hearing aid style should match your hearing test results. It should also match how you live, work, and move through the day.
A good fit starts with a full hearing test and a real conversation. That is how the “best on paper” option becomes the best option for you.
The Main Hearing Aid Styles
Most hearing aids fall into two broad groups.
Some styles sit behind the ear. Others sit in or near the ear canal.
Your audiologist will show you what each one feels like and how it works, then guide you toward the right match.
Behind-the-Ear (BTE)
A behind-the-ear hearing aid sits behind the ear. Sound travels through a small tube to an earmold or ear tip.
This style works for many levels of hearing loss. It also provides a stable fit for active days.
Many people like BTE styles because they feel secure. Cleaning can also feel simpler because the parts are easier to see and handle.
Receiver-in-Canal (RIC)
A receiver-in-canal style sits behind the ear, with a thin wire that connects to a small speaker in the ear canal.
RIC styles are popular because they feel light and open. Many people say the sound feels natural, especially for mild to moderate hearing loss.
This style can also offer strong features for speech in noise and phone streaming. Your audiologist will help you choose the right receiver strength for your hearing needs.
In-the-Ear (ITE)
An in-the-ear hearing aid sits inside the outer ear. The faceplate is visible, and the hearing aid is one piece.
Some patients like ITE styles because they are easier to insert than smaller in-canal options. Handling can be simpler for people with limited finger strength.
ITE styles also keep everything in one place. That can feel less fussy for daily use.
In-the-Canal (ITC)
An in-the-canal hearing aid sits partly in the ear canal. It is smaller than an ITE and less visible for many people.
Comfort and fit depend on your ear shape. Your audiologist will check if your ear canal size supports this option.
ITC styles can work well for people who want a smaller device while still keeping handling reasonable.
Completely-in-Canal (CIC)
A completely-in-canal hearing aid sits deeper in the ear canal and is designed to be less noticeable.
This style can be a good match for some people who want a discreet option. It requires good finger control for insertion and removal.
CIC styles also depend on ear canal shape and hearing needs. Your audiologist will confirm if this option fits your hearing profile.
Hearing Aid Style Choice Is Not Only About Looks
Many people start with “I want the smallest one.” That makes sense.
However, daily success depends on comfort, clear sound, and easy use. A hearing aid that is hard to handle or does not match your hearing needs leads to less wear time.
More wear time leads to better results. Our audiologists in Littleton, CO and Arvada, CO will help you choose a style you will use every day.
Your Hearing Needs + Your Routine = The Right Style
Style choice is part hearing, part lifestyle. When choosing the best hearing aid style, we need to consider the following:
Dexterity and vision
Small batteries and small buttons can be tough. Rechargeable options and larger styles can make daily use easier.
Glasses and masks
Behind-the-ear styles can still work well with glasses. Your audiologist can adjust the fit and show you how to put them on in the right order.
Work and hobbies
Meetings, phone calls, and noisy jobs require strong speech support. Music, fitness, and outdoor time can affect comfort needs.
Ear shape and comfort
Some ears do best with soft tips. Others need custom earmolds for a secure seal and stable sound.
Your provider brings all of this together into one clear recommendation.
Features That Pair With Different Styles
Not every feature is available in every style. Your hearing needs also matter.
Rechargeable power
Many behind-the-ear and RIC options support rechargeable batteries. This reduces battery changes and makes daily use easier.
Bluetooth streaming
Phone calls and media streaming are common in modern hearing aids. Style and brand can affect how strong and stable streaming feels.
Speech support in noise
Noise control features help in restaurants and group talk. Your audiologist will match these tools to your hearing test results and listening goals.
Tinnitus support
Some hearing aids include sound options for tinnitus relief. This is most helpful when it is paired with a tinnitus plan and follow-up care.
Your plan should include more than a device choice. It should include proper fitting, fine-tuning, and support over time.
Why Hearing Aid Fitting and Verification Matter
The same hearing aid can sound different in different ears. Ear shape changes how sound travels.
New Leaf Hearing Care uses professional fitting and verification, including Real Ear Measurements. This confirms that your hearing aids match your hearing needs in your ears, not in a lab.
This step helps speech sound clearer. It also helps the volume feel more comfortable.

After selection, we fit the devices, program them, and teach you how to use and care for them. Follow-up visits give you fine-tuning and support as your brain adjusts to new sounds.
Why Local Audiology Care Helps
A hearing aid is not a one-time purchase. It is a health tool that needs long-term support.

Both our locations offer the same patient-first approach. Littleton patients who knew Columbine Hearing Care will recognize the same commitment to quality care.

Receiver-in-canal (RIC) hearing aids are one of the most common styles because they feel light, sound natural for many patients, and support modern features like streaming.

Your audiologist uses your hearing test results, ear shape, comfort needs, and daily listening goals to recommend the hearing aid style that fits your life and your hearing level.

Behind-the-ear (BTE) and in-the-ear (ITE) styles are often easier to handle because they are larger and simpler to insert, especially for people with limited finger strength.

children.text: Hearing loss facts are clear, plain-language truths about how hearing loss starts, how it affects daily life, and what care helps.
Knowing the facts about hearing loss helps you spot early signs, avoid delays, and get the right plan from an audiologist instead of guessing.
New Leaf Hearing Care shares trusted hearing loss facts and provides hearing care in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.
Most people do not notice hearing loss all at once. It shows up in small ways, then it starts to change how life feels.
These hearing loss facts help you understand what is happening, what causes it, and why early care matters. A little knowledge can save years of frustration.
New Leaf Hearing Care serves patients in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO with hearing tests, hearing aid care, and long-term support.
Fact 1: Hearing Loss is Common
Hearing loss affects people of many ages, not just older adults. Many adults report trouble hearing, even when they still “get by” day to day.
Because it is common, it deserves routine care. A hearing test gives clear answers and a starting point.
New Leaf Hearing Care provides hearing tests in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care).
Fact 2: Hearing Loss is Not Just “Volume”
Many people can hear sounds but still miss words. Voices can seem mumbled, especially in group talk.
This happens because speech has many soft sounds. When those sounds drop out, the brain has to fill in gaps.
That extra work leads to listening fatigue. By the end of the day, conversations can feel tiring.
Fact 3: Noise Can Damage Hearing Without Pain
Loud noise does not always hurt in the moment. Damage can still happen, even when you feel fine.
A CDC report found that many adults who say their hearing is good already have measurable hearing loss.
Noise exposure at home and in the community plays a role. Tools, lawn equipment, concerts, and sporting events all add up.
Fact 4: Ringing in the Ears is a Real Sign
Ringing, buzzing, or humming is called tinnitus. Tinnitus often shows up with hearing loss.
The sound can feel louder in quiet rooms. Sleep can suffer, and focus can drop.
A tinnitus evaluation can identify hearing changes and guide a relief plan. Counseling and sound support can lower distress.
Fact 5: Untreated Hearing Loss Can Affect Connection
Hearing loss changes how social time feels. People start to avoid noisy places because it takes too much effort.
Research links hearing loss with loneliness and social isolation in older adults.
The good news is that support helps. Hearing care can reduce strain and make it easier to stay engaged.
Fact 6: Children Can Have Hearing Loss, Too
Some children are born with hearing loss. Others develop it later.
NIDCD notes that about 2 to 3 out of every 1,000 children in the U.S. are born with detectable hearing loss in one or both ears.
Early testing supports speech, learning, and confidence in school. If you have concerns, a hearing check is a smart next step.
Fact 7: Hearing Aids are Not “One-Size-Fits-All”
Hearing aids are helpful tools, but results depend on the fit and the setup. A device needs to match your hearing test and your daily goals.
A proper fitting includes programming and follow-up visits. Fine-tuning helps speech feel clearer and comfort feel right.
When care is personal, the experience improves. At New Leaf Hearing Care, we want you to feel supported, not rushed.
Fact 8: The Brain is Part of Hearing
Your brain turns sound into meaning. That is why speech in noise feels hard when hearing changes start.
Some patients benefit from training tools that build listening skills. Others benefit from communication coaching that reduces frustration at home.
A full plan looks at real life, not just a chart.
Fact 9: Prevention Protects Hearing
Hearing damage is easier to prevent than to undo. Simple habits protect hearing over time.
Use ear protection for loud tools and live music. Keep headphone volume at a safe level.
Take breaks from loud settings. Distance from the sound source also helps.
If you want a clear plan, an audiologist can guide hearing protection that fits your lifestyle.
New Leaf Hearing Care connects you to trusted audiologists in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.
Fact 10: A Hearing Test Gives You Control
A hearing test tells you what is happening now. It also helps you track changes later.
Testing is also useful when symptoms feel sudden. Earwax buildup can cause a fast drop in hearing, and removal can restore sound quickly.
If hearing loss is present, testing guides your next step. That next step can be monitoring, treatment, or hearing technology support.
Why Choose New Leaf Hearing Care
Hearing care should support a full, active life. Our approach at New Leaf Hearing Care is focused on wellness, connection, and long-term guidance.
Appointments are designed to be clear and personal. You get answers in plain language and a plan you can follow.
You can consult with our audiologists in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO. Feel free to choose the location that fits your schedule.

How do I know if I have hearing loss?
Common signs include asking people to repeat themselves, trouble hearing in noise, turning the TV up, and feeling tired after conversations; a hearing test confirms if a hearing loss is present.
How does a hearing test work?
A hearing test measures how softly you hear sounds and how clearly you understand speech. Our audiologist explains the results and recommends next steps.
How do I schedule a hearing test in Arvada or Littleton?

Common Signs of Hearing Loss
Many people live with hearing loss longer than they realize. The brain gets good at filling in gaps until it becomes too hard.
These signs are common:
Speech sounds unclear, even when it is loud enough.
Noise in restaurants makes it hard to follow the conversation.
You miss soft voices, doorbells, or timers.
Family members say you ask “what?” more than before.
You feel worn out after social time.
If these sound familiar, a hearing test is the next step.
Why Hearing Loss Feels Worse in Noise
Background noise hides speech. Distance also makes voices softer, even when people speak clearly.
Hearing loss removes certain speech sounds first.
When those sounds drop out, the brain has to guess.
That extra effort can lead to fatigue and frustration. Hearing care aims to make listening easier again.
What Causes Hearing Loss
Different causes lead to different solutions. A professional exam helps identify the reason.
Common causes include:
Age-related hearing changes
Hearing can shift slowly over time. Many people notice it first in group settings.
Noise exposure
Tools, loud music, and job-site noise can damage hearing over the years. Noise damage can build without pain.
Earwax buildup
Wax can block sound and cause a sudden change in hearing. Safe removal can bring quick relief.
Ear pressure or middle ear issues
Fluid and pressure changes can reduce hearing and create a “plugged” feeling.
Tinnitus and hearing loss together
Ringing or buzzing is common when hearing changes are present. A tinnitus plan can be added when needed.
A hearing test separates these possibilities and guides the next step.

What to Expect at Your Hearing Evaluation
Your visit starts with a conversation. We want to know what you notice and which moments feel hardest.
An ear inspection (otoscopy) comes next. This helps identify wax or irritation that can block sound.
Testing then measures hearing abilities across pitches and volumes. Speech testing is included because understanding words is the goal.
Results are reviewed in plain language. You leave with clear answers and a plan you can act on.
What a Hearing Test Can Tell You
A hearing test provides useful detail. It can show:
Which sounds you miss most
How well you understand speech
How each ear compares to the other
What level of support will help
This information shapes the treatment plan, keeps care focused, and prevents wasted time and money.
Hearing Loss Treatment Options
Treatment should match your life, not a generic checklist. Your plan depends on test results and your goals.
Options can include:
Monitoring and prevention
If changes are mild, a baseline and follow-up schedule can protect your hearing long term. Hearing protection may be recommended for loud work or hobbies.

Hearing aids support clearer speech and reduce listening strain. A fitting and follow-up fine-tuning help your brain adjust to new sound.
Assistive listening tools
TV streamers and remote microphones can improve speech in noise and over distance. These tools help in meetings, restaurants, and family gatherings.
