Some patients benefit from coaching that improves daily communication. Training tools can strengthen listening skills and improve confidence in tough settings.
Tinnitus evaluation and counseling
If tinnitus is part of your concern, counseling and sound-based support can reduce the impact and improve comfort.
Our Littleton, CO or Arvada, CO audiologists will guide the best combination. The goal is a plan you will use, not a plan that sits on paper.
Why Seeing an Audiologist Matters
Online tests and quick screenings cannot look in your ears. They also cannot measure hearing in a controlled way.
An audiologist provides accurate testing and a full review of your symptoms. That includes guidance on medical referral when certain warning signs are present.
Professional care also provides long-term support. Hearing needs change, and your plan should keep up.
Hearing Wellness for a Full, Active Life
Hearing loss affects more than the ears. It affects relationships, confidence, and how connected you feel to daily life.
New Leaf Hearing Care takes time to listen and educate. Recommendations are built around your needs, lifestyle, and goals.
You get support that helps you hear better now and stay engaged for years to come.

Hearing loss is a reduced ability to hear sounds or understand speech, often showing up first as trouble in noise or group conversations, or with soft voices.

Common signs include turning the TV up, asking people to repeat themselves, struggling in restaurants, and feeling tired after conversations; a hearing test confirms it.

children.text: How we hear is the process of the ear and brain working together to turn sound waves into meaning.
When one part of the system is not working well, speech becomes harder to follow, and an audiologist can find the cause and guide the right care.
New Leaf Hearing Care explains how we hear and offers hearing care in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care).
Many people think hearing is only about the ears. The truth is more interesting. How we hear depends on your ears and your brain working as a team.
When sound feels muffled or speech feels unclear, that team is not getting the full signal. A hearing test can show where the breakdown is and what to do next.
New Leaf Hearing Care helps patients in Arvada and Littleton, CO understand their hearing and take the next step with confidence.
Sound Starts as Vibration
Sound is a vibration that moves through the air. Your ear collects that vibration and funnels  it into the ear canal.
Even a soft voice creates movement in the air. Your body picks up that movement and turns it into sound you can recognize.
The Outer Ear: Your Sound Funnel
The outer ear is the part you can see. It helps guide sound into the ear canal.
The ear canal carries sound to the eardrum. The eardrum is a thin layer of skin that moves when sound hits it.
That movement is the first big step in hearing.
The Middle Ear: Small Bones Doing Big Work
Behind the eardrum are three tiny bones called ossicles. They move with the eardrum and pass sound along.
These bones boost the vibration and make sound strong enough to move into the inner ear.
Pressure and fluid in the middle ear can affect this step. When the middle ear is not working well, sound can feel blocked.
The Inner Ear: Where Sound Becomes a Signal
The inner ear has a small, curled organ called the cochlea. Inside it are tiny hair cells.
These hair cells react to sound and turn it into signals. Those signals travel through a nerve to the brain.
Hair cells are delicate. Loud noise, aging, and some health issues can damage them.
When hair cells are damaged, sound can feel less clear, even when it is loud enough.
The Brain: Where Hearing Becomes Understanding
Your brain is the part that gives sound meaning. It sorts, filters, and fills in details.
In a quiet room, the brain has an easier job. In a noisy room, it has to work harder to pull speech out of the background.
That is why many people say, “I can hear people talking, but I can’t understand the words.”
It is not a sign of laziness or lack of focus. It is a sign that the brain is missing parts of the sound signal.
Why Hearing in Noise Feels So Hard
Background noise masks speech. Distance makes voices softer. Fast speech reduces the time to catch each word.
When hearing changes are present, the brain uses extra energy to keep up. That leads to listening fatigue.
A full hearing plan aims to lower that effort. You should not feel drained after a simple conversation.
Common Reasons for Hearing Changes
Hearing can change for different reasons. A hearing test helps sort them out.
Earwax buildup can block sound and cause a sudden drop in hearing. This can be fixed with safe in-office care.
Middle ear problems can create pressure and reduce hearing for a period of time.
Inner ear hearing loss is common as we age. This can also happen after loud noise exposure.
Tinnitus can show up with hearing loss. Ringing or buzzing can make it harder to focus and rest.
Each cause has a different solution. That is why professional testing matters.
Why Online Tests Fall Short
Online hearing screenings do not look in your ears and cannot check your middle ear function.
They also do not measure hearing in a controlled way. The results can be misleading.
A clinical hearing test gives you clear numbers and a clear meaning. You also get a plan you can act on.
What a Hearing Test Measures
A comprehensive hearing test checks how you hear different pitches and volumes. It also checks how you understand speech.
Results can show if one ear is stronger than the other. They also show which sounds you miss most.
That detail matters because speech is built from many small sounds. Missing a few can change the whole message.
New Leaf Hearing Care provides hearing tests in Littleton, CO and Arvada, CO.
How Hearing Aids Help the Hearing System
Hearing aids give the brain a stronger, clearer signal. They amplify the sounds you miss and shape sound for speech.
A hearing aid also supports comfort. The goal is not “louder;” the goal is “clearer and easier.”
Fit and programming matter. A proper fitting helps sound feel natural and reduces whistling.
More Than Devices: Hearing Wellness
New Leaf Hearing Care focuses on hearing wellness for a full, active life. That includes support beyond devices.
Some people benefit from communication training, which builds better habits for daily conversations. Others benefit from auditory training tools that strengthen listening skills.
Counseling can also help, especially when tinnitus or stress is part of the picture. A strong plan supports the whole person.
When to Schedule an Appointment
Small signs are easy to dismiss. Over time, those signs can lead to less connection and more effort.
Book a visit at New Leaf Hearing Care if sounds and conversations become unclear. Come in if you keep turning the TV up. Reach out if noise makes speech hard to follow.
Early care gives you more choices and faster relief.
FAQs
How do I know if my problem is hearing loss or earwax?
Earwax often causes a sudden muffled feeling or pressure, while hearing loss tends to build over time. An in-office ear check and hearing test confirm the cause.
How does the brain affect hearing?
The brain sorts sounds, focuses on speech, and helps you understand words, especially in noise. When the brain gets a weak sound signal, understanding speech becomes harder.
How do I schedule a hearing test at New Leaf Hearing Care?

You can schedule by calling the clinic or booking online for an appointment at our Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO clinic (formerly Columbine Hearing Care). Our audiologist will guide you through testing and the next steps.
Schedule A Hearing Test in Colorado
Understanding how we hear makes it easier to act when something feels off. Hearing changes do not have to shrink your world.
Schedule a visit with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care).
A professional hearing exam gives clear answers, and a plan built by an audiologist helps you hear and live with more ease.

children.text: At New Leaf Hearing Care, our patients are our top priority. We work hard to provide excellent hearing healthcare while also keeping your budget in mind. We know hearing care can feel expensive, so we offer clear pricing and flexible payment options to make your visit as simple and affordable as possible.
Our clinics serve Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care).
Do We Bill Insurance?
In general, insurance does not cover every service an audiologist may recommend.
Medicare

New Leaf Hearing Care does not participate in Medicare.Medicare Part B does not pay for routine hearing tests, hearing tests for hearing aids, advanced diagnostic testing, new hearing aids, services related to hearing aids, or earwax removal performed by an audiologist.
Commercial Insurance
We are out-of-network with all commercial insurance carriers due to low reimbursement rates. This allows us to offer the same affordable pricing to every patient.
“Hearing Aid Benefits” and Discount Programs

Some commercial plans (including some Medicare Advantage plans) advertise hearing aid “benefits.” In many cases, these are discount programs managed by a third-party vendor that controls pricing, device options, where you can receive care, and how many follow-up visits you get.
We do not participate in these programs because they can limit the quality of care we can provide.
What This Means for You
New Leaf Hearing Care is not in-network with Medicare, Medicare Advantage plans, or commercial insurance carriers.
You will receive an itemized invoice that you can submit to your insurance company for possible out-of-network reimbursement.

We understand patients want to use insurance. Based on long experience in hearing healthcare, insurance “coverage” for hearing services (especially hearing aids) often falls short. Staying out-of-network helps us keep our pricing lower, and we can frequently match or beat many “discounted” plan prices.
Fees for Services
Because our services are private pay, we aim to be fully transparent about costs and provide an itemized list of products and services.
(This can link to the pricing page: “View Our Price List”.)
Fees for Hearing Loss Treatment
Treating hearing loss involves more than purchasing hearing aids. It includes evaluation, assessment, fitting, fine-tuning, and ongoing maintenance that support the best outcomes.
We use a bundled treatment model that includes products, visits, and services for a set amount of time, with three payment options:
Pay-in-Full Treatment Program

Financed Treatment Program
Financing options through Cherry
Low monthly payments with no interest for 15 months (with approved credit)
Hear It Forward Membership
$999 Start-up fee, then $127/month
Premium hearing devices upgraded to new technology every 3 years
All fitting and follow-up care included
CLICK HERE FOR MORE DETAILS (link to Hear it Forward Page)
Flexible Financing
We offer flexible financing through Cherry (with approved credit) so you can choose hearing technology that matches your needs and lifestyle. (include link to Cherry page)
Treatment for All
We never want cost to be the reason someone goes without hearing care. Our nonprofit, Hearing the Call – Colorado, is one option that helps community members who cannot afford treatment.
No Surprises Act
New Leaf Hearing Care is committed to complying with the federal No Surprises Act and Colorado regulations related to surprise billing and balance billing.
For questions or concerns, federal contact options include FederalPPDRQuestions@cms.hhs.gov and 800-985-3059.

children.text: LACE AI Pro is an app-based auditory training program that strengthens how your brain understands speech, including speech in noise and fast talk.
Hearing devices boost sound, and LACE AI Pro builds stronger listening skills so conversations feel clearer and less tiring.
New Leaf Hearing Care provides guided LACE AI Pro auditory training in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.

LACE AI Pro is a smart way to build better listening, even when you already wear hearing aids. It is a training program you use at home on your phone or tablet, with guidance from your audiology team.
At New Leaf Hearing Care, we focus on hearing wellness for a full, active life. Strong hearing supports brain health, close relationships, and daily confidence.
You can start LACE AI Pro through our Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO office. Our team helps you set it up, stay on track, and use it as part of a full hearing plan.
Why Hearing Aids Are Not the Whole Answer
Hearing aids make sounds louder and clearer. Your brain still has to sort those sounds and turn them into meaning.
That is why many people say, “I hear you, but I don’t understand you.” The problem shows up most in restaurants, group talks, and meetings.
LACE AI Pro trains the brain skills that support speech understanding. It helps you do better in the real places where listening feels hardest.
What LACE AI Pro Trains
LACE AI Pro focuses on key listening skills that affect daily communication.
Speech in noise
You practice hearing speech while background sounds are present. This matches real life, where rooms are rarely quiet.
Fast speech and clarity
You learn to catch words when someone talks quickly or turns their head.
Focus and listening memory
You build the mental skills that help you hold onto what you heard and respond with confidence.
These skills support better communication with family, friends, and coworkers.
What You Can Expect from the Program
LACE AI Pro works best with steady practice. Most patients do short sessions that fit into a normal day.
The app adjusts as you improve. You get training that matches your level, so the work stays useful and not overwhelming.
Many people like that the lessons feel simple and clear. The goal is progress you can notice in daily talk, not homework that sits on a shelf.
How New Leaf Hearing Care Supports You
You do not have to figure out how to use your hearing aids or how to sort out your hearing problems alone.
Our team at New Leaf Hearing Care helps you connect LACE AI Pro to your hearing plan.
We start with a consultation so we understand your hearing goals. We also review your hearing test results and your daily listening needs.
Then we help you set up the program and show you how to use it the right way.
We can track progress and coach you through common roadblocks, like noisy practice spaces or uneven device settings.
This guided approach helps you get more value from your training time.
Who Is a Good Fit for LACE AI Pro
LACE AI Pro is a strong choice for many patients, including:
People who struggle in noise, even with hearing aids.
New hearing aid users who want faster real-life success.
Long-time hearing aid users who feel stuck in group talks.
Adults with busy work lives who need clearer speech in meetings.
People who want less listening fatigue at the end of the day.
Your audiologist will tell you if LACE AI Pro fits your needs.
Expert guidance from an audiologist matters because training should match your hearing profile and your goals.
LACE AI Pro as Part of Hearing Wellness
At New Leaf Hearing Care, we take a whole-person approach. Hearing is linked to connection, confidence, and daily life.
LACE AI Pro supports that wellness plan by building stronger listening habits. It also helps you get more use from hearing aids and other hearing tools.
If your goal is clearer conversations and less strain, this program is a practical next step.
Frequently Asked Questions
What is LACE AI Pro?
LACE AI Pro is an app-based auditory training program that improves speech understanding by training the brain skills used in real conversations, including speech in noise.
How does LACE AI Pro work with hearing aids?
LACE AI Pro works alongside hearing aids by improving how you process the sound your devices deliver, so speech feels clearer and listening takes less effort.
How do I get started with LACE AI Pro at New Leaf Hearing Care?
You schedule a consultation in Arvada or Littleton, review your hearing needs with an audiologist, and then get help setting up LACE AI Pro with a training plan you can follow at home.
How do I practice LACE AI Pro the right way?
You practice in a quiet space, use the device setup recommended by your audiologist, and follow the training plan consistently so your progress builds week by week.
Schedule a LACE AI Pro Consultation in Colorado
Good hearing is more than volume. Strong listening skills help you stay engaged in the life you love.
It’s time to take the next step toward clearer conversations.
Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada or Littleton, CO, and ask about LACE AI Pro.
With the right support and steady training, LACE AI Pro can help conversations feel clearer, calmer, and easier to follow.

children.text: Online hearing tests are quick at-home screenings that give a rough idea of your hearing ability using a phone or computer.
Results can point to a problem, but only an audiologist can confirm the cause and build a care plan you can trust.
New Leaf Hearing Care helps patients after online hearing tests with professional hearing care in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.
Online hearing tests are easy to access. Many people take one after they notice the TV getting louder, speech sounding unclear, or noise making conversations hard.
These tools can be a helpful first step. Online hearing tests can also create false worry or false relief, depending on the device, the room, and the volume settings.
New Leaf Hearing Care helps you move from a quick screen to clear answers. Hearing tests and audiologist consultations are available in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care).
What Online Hearing Tests Can Do Well
Online tests work best as a simple check. They can help you notice a possible issue sooner.
A good online test can show that you miss certain tones. It can also highlight trouble with speech sounds.
That signal can push you to take the next step. For many people, that next step is the first real hearing exam they have had in years.
Why Online Results Can Be Wrong
Online tests are not done in a controlled setting. Sound can change from one phone to the next.
Room noise changes results fast.
A fan, air vent, traffic, or a nearby TV can hide test tones.
Headphones also matter. Cheap earbuds and loose-fitting headphones can change what you hear during the test.
Volume settings create another problem. Too high or too low can make the result meaningless.
What Online Hearing Tests Cannot Measure
Hearing is more than “can you hear a beep.” A full hearing evaluation checks the whole system.
Online tests do not look in your ears.
Wax buildup can block sound and mimic the feeling of hearing loss.
Online tests do not check middle-ear function.
Fluid or pressure problems can affect hearing and comfort.
Online tests do not measure speech understanding in the same way a clinic can.
Many people struggle most with speech, not tones.
Online tests also do not help rule out medical red flags.
Sudden hearing loss, one-sided changes, pain, drainage, or dizziness need prompt care.
A Helpful Way to Use Online Tests
Online tests are useful when you treat them like a screen. They can help you decide if it is time to get checked.
If your result shows concern, book an in-person exam. If your result looks normal but daily life still feels hard, book an exam anyway.
Your experience matters more than a score. People live with hearing trouble for years while still “passing” quick screens.
Signs You Should See an Audiologist
A hearing exam is the right move when hearing everyday sounds or conversations are getting harder.
You should see an audiologist if:
Speech sounds unclear in restaurants or group dinners.
You rely on captions more than you used to.
You feel tired after conversations.
You miss doorbells, timers, or soft voices.
Family members say you misunderstand them.
Ringing or buzzing is present, especially at night.
These signs point to a need for real testing and real guidance.
What an In-Person Hearing Test Includes
At New Leaf Hearing Care, a hearing evaluation starts with a conversation. We want to know what you hear, what you miss, and where you struggle most.
Next comes an ear inspection (otoscopy). This helps identify wax and other issues that can block sound.
We then complete a full hearing test with professional equipment. Speech testing is included, because understanding words is the goal.
You leave with results explained properly and a plan that fits your needs.
What Your Results Can Tell You
A clinical test shows more than “yes or no.” It shows patterns that guide smart care.
Testing can identify which pitches you miss. That matters because speech is made of many small sounds.
Results can also show if one ear is different. Asymmetry can change treatment and referral decisions.
Your audiologist can then connect the results to your life. That means specific guidance for work calls, restaurants, family time, and social events.
What Happens After Hearing Testing
Some patients need simple care first. Wax removal can restore hearing quickly when wax is the cause.
Other patients need a hearing wellness plan. That may include hearing aids, assistive listening devices, or training tools that build listening skills.
If tinnitus is part of the picture, tinnitus counseling and sound support can be added to the plan.
The goal stays the same. Better communication and less effort during everyday life.
New Leaf Hearing Care offers a wide selection of hearing solutions, hearing aids, and communication programs provided by trusted audiologists in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.
Why Local Audiology Care Makes a Difference
Online tests cannot adjust to your needs, but a clinic visit can.
An audiologist takes time to listen. Your care plan is built around your goals, not a generic result page.
Follow-up matters too. Hearing changes over time, and your plan should keep up.
That long-term relationship is what helps people stay confident and engaged.
Schedule a Hearing Exam in Arvada or Littleton
If you took an online test because something felt off, trust that instinct. Getting checked is the fastest way to replace worry with clear answers.
New Leaf Hearing Care serves Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO with patient-first, wellness-focused hearing care.
A professional visit is the step that turns information into a plan you can use.
FAQs
How do I know if my online hearing test result is accurate?
Accuracy depends on a quiet room, good headphones, correct volume, and a calibrated device, so the only way to confirm the result is an in-person hearing evaluation with an audiologist.
How does an in-person hearing test compare to an online test?
An in-person test checks your ears, measures hearing with calibrated equipment, tests speech understanding, and provides a clear diagnosis and treatment plan based on your needs.
How do I schedule a hearing test after taking an online test?
You can schedule a hearing exam with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada or Littleton by calling the office or booking online, and the team will guide you to the right appointment type.
Take the Next Step After Online Hearing Tests

Online screens can raise helpful questions, but they cannot give a full answer. The best next step is a hearing evaluation that checks your ears, your hearing, and your speech understanding in a controlled setting.

If you have concerns after online hearing tests, or if you would like to get a professional hearing test at an audiology clinic, schedule a hearing test with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO.

children.text: This Privacy Policy describes how your personal information is collected, used, and shared when you visit or make a purchase from https://columbinecares.com/ (the “Site”).
PERSONAL INFORMATION WE COLLECT

When you visit the Site, we automatically collect certain information about your device, including information about your web browser, IP address, time zone, and some of the cookies that are installed on your device. Additionally, as you browse the Site, we collect information about the individual web pages or products that you view, what websites or search terms referred you to the Site, and information about how you interact with the Site. We refer to this automatically collected information as “Device Information.”
We collect Device Information using the following technologies:

– “Cookies” are data files that are placed on your device or computer and often include an anonymous unique identifier. For more information about cookies, and how to disable cookies, visit http://www.allaboutcookies.org
– “Log files” track actions occurring on the Site, and collect data including your IP address, browser type, Internet service provider, referring/exit pages, and date/time stamps.
– “Web beacons,” “tags,” and “pixels” are electronic files used to record information about how you browse the Site.

Additionally, when you make a purchase or attempt to make a purchase through the Site, we collect certain information from you, including your name, billing address, shipping address, payment information (including credit card numbers), email address, and phone number.  We refer to this information as “Order Information.”
When we talk about “Personal Information” in this Privacy Policy, we are talking both about Device Information and Order Information.

We use the Device Information that we collect to help us screen for potential risk and fraud (in particular, your IP address), and more generally to improve and optimize our Site (for example, by generating analytics about how our customers browse and interact with the Site, and to assess the success of our marketing and advertising campaigns).
We share your Personal Information with third parties to help us use your Personal Information, as described above.

We also use Google Analytics to help us understand how our customers use the Site–you can read more about how Google uses your Personal Information here:  https://www.google.com/intl/en/policies/privacy/.
You can also opt out of Google Analytics here:  https://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout.

Finally, we may also share your Personal Information to comply with applicable laws and regulations, to respond to a subpoena, search warrant, or other lawful request for information we receive, or to otherwise protect our rights.
BEHAVIORAL ADVERTISING

As described above, we use your Personal Information to provide you with targeted advertisements or marketing communications we believe may be of interest to you.  For more information about how targeted advertising works, you can visit the Network Advertising Initiative’s (“NAI”) educational page at http://www.networkadvertising.org/understanding-online-advertising/how-does-it-work.
You can opt out of targeted advertising by:
FACEBOOK – https://www.facebook.com/settings/?tab=ads
GOOGLE – https://www.google.com/settings/ads/anonymous
BING – https://advertise.bingads.microsoft.com/en-us/resources/policies/personalized-ads
Additionally, you can opt out of some of these services by visiting the Digital Advertising Alliance’s opt-out portal at:  http://optout.aboutads.info/.
DO NOT TRACK
Please note that we do not alter our Site’s data collection and use practices when we see a Do Not Track signal from your browser.
DATA RETENTION
When you place an order through the Site, we will maintain your Order Information for our records unless and until you ask us to delete this information.
CHANGES
We may update this privacy policy from time to time in order to reflect, for example, changes to our practices or for other operational, legal, or regulatory reasons.
CONTACT US

For more information about our privacy practices, if you have questions, or if you would like to make a complaint, please contact us by e-mail at littleton@newleafhearing.com or by mail using the details provided below:
5808 S Rapp St, Ste 102, Littleton, CO 80120

children.text: Protecting your hearing means reducing damage from loud noise, illness, and injury through smart habits and the right hearing protection.
Hearing damage builds over time, and early prevention keeps conversations easier, protects brain health, and lowers long-term hearing risk.
New Leaf Hearing Care helps you protect your hearing with expert guidance in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.
Many people wait until hearing feels “bad enough” to act. Hearing damage is easier to prevent than to reverse, so it pays to protect your hearing early.
New Leaf Hearing Care helps you understand your risk, choose the right protection, and build simple habits that fit your life. You get guidance from hearing experts, not guesswork from the internet.
Visit us in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO (formerly Columbine Hearing Care) for hearing support built around wellness, connection, and an active life.
Why Hearing Protection Matters in Daily Life
Hearing is not only about volume. Hearing is how you stay connected to people, work, and the sounds that make life feel normal.
When hearing drops, speech becomes harder to follow. Noise in restaurants starts to win. Phone calls feel tiring.
Prevention protects more than your ears. It protects your energy, your focus, and your confidence in conversation.
Loud Noise Is a Common Cause of Hearing Loss
Noise damage can happen at a concert. It can also happen at work, in a shop, on a mower, or at a game.
The tricky part is that loud noise does not always feel painful. Many people stay in a loud setting because it feels “fine.”
Your ears still take the hit. Repeated exposure can lead to permanent hearing loss and ringing in the ears.
Signs You Are Around Too Much Noise
A few clues can tell you when sound is too loud:
You have to raise your voice to talk to someone close by.
Your ears ring after you leave the area.
Sounds feel dull or your ears feel “plugged” for hours after.
You feel worn out after time in loud places.
These signs mean your ears need protection. They also mean it is time to check your hearing.
Simple Ways to Protect Your Hearing
Hearing protection works best when it is easy to use. A plan that fits your routine is the plan you follow.
Try these steps:
Lower the volume on personal audio.
Take short breaks from loud places.
Move away from speakers and loud tools.
Wear earplugs or earmuffs during loud tasks.
Keep a spare pair of earplugs in your car or bag.
Small changes reduce risk. Consistent protection reduces it even more.
Why “Any Earplug” Is Not Always Enough
Store-bought foam plugs help, but they are not the best fit for every ear. Some people struggle with comfort. Others struggle with getting them in the right way.
A poor fit can leak sound. That leak lowers protection.
Custom earplugs solve this problem for many people. They are made to fit your ear shape, so they seal well and feel more comfortable.
Custom Hearing Protection for Real Life
New Leaf Hearing Care can help you choose custom hearing protection based on what you do each week.
Common needs include:
Work noise
Trades, shops, and job sites can expose you to loud sound for hours. Custom plugs can protect hearing while still allowing speech to come through.
Music and concert
Music-focused earplugs reduce volume while keeping sound quality clear. Many people like this because music still feels like music.
Hunting and shooting sports
Impulse sounds are intense and damaging. Specialty options can protect against those peaks while supporting awareness.
Motor sports and outdoor equipment
Motorcycles, snow blowers, and leaf blowers create steady noise. Protection reduces strain and protects hearing over time.
Sleep and sound sensitivity
Some people use plugs to sleep better or reduce sound stress. Fit matters here, since comfort is the whole point.
A visit to New Leaf Hearing Care can identify the best style for your needs. Your ears and your lifestyle guide the choice.
Illness and Infection Can Affect Hearing Too
Noise is not the only risk. Some illnesses can harm hearing when they are not treated.
Ear infections can affect hearing for a time. Long-term health issues can also impact the ear and balance system.
Fast care matters when symptoms show up. If you notice sudden hearing changes, ear pain, drainage, or dizziness, get help right away.
Our Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO audiologists can check hearing and guide the next medical step, if needed.
Injury and Trauma Can Change Hearing
The hearing system is sensitive. A blow to the head, a neck injury, or sudden pressure changes can affect hearing and balance.
Not every injury can be prevented. Smart safety habits still reduce risk.
Helmets, seatbelts, and safe work practices protect your whole body, including the systems that support hearing.
Medication Side Effects and Hearing
Some medicines can affect hearing and balance. People can notice ringing, dizziness, or changes in hearing after starting a new drug.
Talk with your prescribing provider about side effects. Tell your audiologist if symptoms start after a medication change.
A hearing test gives clear data. That helps guide next steps with your medical team.
How an Audiologist Helps You Protect Your Hearing
Protecting hearing works best with a plan. An audiologist helps you build it with the right tools and clear steps.
At New Leaf Hearing Care, support can include:
A hearing test to set a baseline.
Counseling on noise risk and safe habits.
Custom hearing protection options.
Follow-up testing to track changes over time.
This is not a one-time conversation. Ongoing care helps you stay ahead of hearing changes.
Hearing Checks Make Prevention Stronger
Many people do not know their hearing has changed until they feel stuck in noise. Regular testing helps you catch changes sooner.
Early detection gives you more options. It also helps you adjust habits before the problem grows.
If you work in noise, hunt, attend concerts, or use loud tools, annual testing is a smart choice.
Protect Your Hearing at Any Age
Hearing protection is not only for older adults. Kids and teens face loud sounds at sporting events, through headphones, and at school activities.
Adults face steady noise from work, tools, and busy schedules. Many also face stress and poor sleep, which can make tinnitus feel louder.
Older adults face age-related changes plus years of sound exposure. Prevention still helps at every stage.
A hearing plan should match your season of life. That is what we build with you.
Why Patients Choose New Leaf Hearing Care
Our approach is centered on wellness and real-life results. We take time to listen and explain options in plain language.
You get guidance based on your needs, not a sales quota. Every recommendation is tied to your goals and daily routine.
Two locations make care easier to access. Visits can be scheduled in Arvada or Littleton, CO.
FAQs
What is the best way to protect your hearing from loud noise?

The best way to protect your hearing from loud noise is to reduce exposure time, increase distance from the sound source, and wear proper hearing protection like earplugs or earmuffs during loud activities.
How do I know if I need custom earplugs?
You need custom earplugs if store-bought plugs feel uncomfortable, fall out, or do not reduce noise enough for your work, hobbies, or sound sensitivity.
How does a hearing test help protect my hearing?
A hearing test gives a baseline of your hearing now and helps catch small changes early, so you can adjust habits and protection before hearing loss becomes more noticeable.
How do I protect my hearing if I already have hearing loss?
You protect your hearing by preventing further damage with ear protection in loud settings and by working with an audiologist to monitor hearing and support safer listening habits.
Schedule a Hearing Protection Visit
Protecting hearing is a practical step you take now, not a problem you solve later. The right plan can prevent years of frustration with noise, missed words, and listening fatigue.
Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada, CO or Littleton, CO.
We’ll help you choose the right protection, set a baseline with testing when needed, and build habits you can follow long term.
If you want to protect your hearing, start with a visit that gives you clear guidance and real tools for daily life.

data.page_title: Research Linking Untreated Hearing Loss to Dementia Risk and Falls
data.meta_title: Hearing Aids and Brain Health: Dementia, Brain Health, and Fall Risk | New Leaf Hearing Care
data.meta_description: Research links untreated hearing loss to dementia and falls. See the evidence and schedule a hearing consult at New Leaf Hearing Care today.

children.text: Research shows that untreated hearing loss increases the risk of cognitive decline and dementia. Multiple large studies, including a landmark randomized clinical trial, demonstrate that hearing aids help protect brain health by keeping the brain stimulated, reducing listening effort, and supporting long-term cognitive function.
Hearing loss is the largest potentially modifiable risk factor for dementia.
Livingston et al.,
The Lancet Commission on Dementia Prevention
Reference
Livingston G, et al.
Dementia prevention, intervention, and care.
The Lancet
Key points:
Identifies
untreated hearing loss as the single largest modifiable risk factor for dementia
Estimates up to
8–9% of dementia cases
could be prevented by addressing hearing loss
Explicitly recommends
hearing aid use
as a preventive strategy
Hearing intervention slowed cognitive decline in older adults at increased risk for dementia.
ACHIEVE Trial – Lin et al.,
The Lancet
Reference
Lin FR, et al.
Hearing intervention versus health education control to reduce cognitive decline in older adults (ACHIEVE).
The Lancet
Key points:
First large
randomized controlled trial
(gold standard)
Showed
48% reduction in cognitive decline
over 3 years in high-risk older adults using hearing aids
Strong evidence that hearing treatment is
protective for brain health
, not just correlated
When the brain receives clearer sound, it stays more active and engaged — reducing cognitive load and decline.
Deal et al.,
Journal of the American Geriatrics Society
Reference
Deal JA, et al.
Hearing impairment and incident dementia and cognitive decline.
Journal of the American Geriatrics Society
Key points:
Longitudinal study following adults over time
Found
lower rates of cognitive decline
in people with treated hearing loss
Helped establish the link between
auditory input and brain stimulation
Research Proving Hearing Aids Support Physical Health And Safety

Research shows that untreated hearing loss increases the risk of falls, balance problems, and physical decline. Hearing aids improve environmental awareness, support balance, and help people stay active, safe, and independent.
Reduced auditory input affects balance cues and situational awareness, increasing fall risk.
Lin & Ferrucci,
_Archives of Internal Medicine_
Reference
Lin FR, Ferrucci L.
Hearing loss and falls among older adults in the United States.
Archives of Internal Medicine
Key points:
Found that adults with hearing loss had a
significantly higher risk of falls
Each 10 dB increase in hearing loss was associated with a
1.4× increased fall risk
Suggests hearing plays a critical role in
spatial awareness, balance, and environmental monitoring
Hearing aids don’t just improve hearing — they help the brain orient the body in space.
Rumalla, Karim, & Hullar, Otology & Neurotology (2015)
Reference
Rumalla K, Karim AM, Hullar TE.
The effect of hearing aids on postural stability.
Otology & Neurotology
Key points:
Demonstrated
improved balance and postural control
when hearing aids were worn
Shows hearing contributes to
multisensory balance integration
, not just communication
One of the strongest studies linking
hearing aid use to physical stability
Treating hearing loss helps people stay active, mobile, and independent longer.
Maharani et al.,
Age and Ageing
Reference
Maharani A, et al.
Longitudinal relationship between hearing aid use and physical functioning.
Age and Ageing
Key points:
Long-term study showing
better physical functioning
in hearing aid users
Hearing aid use was associated with
slower physical decline
over time
Supports the link between hearing, mobility, and independence
Ready to Protect Your Brain Health and Stay Steady on Your Feet?
If you’ve noticed you’re missing words, avoiding noisy places, or feeling more tired after conversations, don’t ignore it.
A professional hearing evaluation can clarify what’s happening and give you a plan that supports clearer communication, stronger confidence, and long-term wellness.
Schedule a hearing consultation with New Leaf Hearing Care in Arvada or Littleton today.

children.text: Tinnitus Evaluation and Management is a clinical service that checks your hearing, reviews your tinnitus, and builds a plan to make ringing less intrusive.
A focused plan can lower stress, improve sleep, and help your brain pay less attention to tinnitus through counseling and sound-based tools.
New Leaf Hearing Care provides Tinnitus Evaluation and Management in Littleton, CO and Arvada, CO.
Tinnitus Evaluation and Management gives you a clear path forward when ringing, buzzing, or humming starts to take over your day. Tinnitus is common, and it can feel loud even in a quiet room.
At New Leaf Hearing Care, we treat tinnitus as a whole-person concern. Your hearing, your stress level, your sleep, and your daily focus all matter.
You can visit us in Littleton, CO or Arvada, CO. Our goal is simple: help tinnitus feel less present, so you can stay connected and confident.
What Tinnitus Feels Like
Tinnitus is a sound you hear that does not come from the outside world. People describe it in different ways.
Some hear ringing. Others notice hissing, clicking, or a steady tone. The sound can be soft, or it can feel sharp and hard to ignore.
Tinnitus can also affect mood. It can push up anxiety, strain focus, and lead to pulling back from social time.
Why a Tinnitus Evaluation Matters
A tinnitus plan works best when it starts with real testing. A quick online quiz cannot do that.
A full tinnitus evaluation checks hearing, looks for patterns, and documents how tinnitus affects your daily life. It also helps your audiologist choose the right tools for relief.
The purpose of evaluation is clarity. You learn what drives your tinnitus and what steps help you regain control.
What Happens During a Tinnitus Evaluation
At New Leaf Hearing Care, your visit begins with a detailed conversation. You talk about:
When tinnitus started.
What it sounds like.
What makes it worse.
How it affects sleep, work, and daily calm.
Next, we complete a full hearing evaluation. Hearing loss and tinnitus are closely linked, so hearing results shape the plan.
We also use extra tools to understand tinnitus. This includes testing, questionnaires, and a review of your health history, medications, and noise exposure.
During the visit, you can also see a demonstration of tinnitus masking devices. That hands-on step helps many patients understand what sound support feels like.
What Tinnitus Management Means
Tinnitus management focuses on reducing how much tinnitus impacts your life. The goal is not to chase silence at all costs.
A strong plan helps your brain treat tinnitus as a low-priority sound. Over time, tinnitus feels less urgent and less distracting.
New Leaf Hearing Care uses a mix of counseling and sound-based strategies to support that shift.
Tinnitus Counseling That Lowers Distress
Tinnitus can trigger strong emotions. That reaction makes the sound feel bigger.

Counseling helps you understand tinnitus, reduce fear, and lower the stress response tied to the sound. This is a key step recognized by leading tinnitus organizations because it helps people change how they react to tinnitus.
In counseling, you get clear answers. You also get practical tools you can use during flare-ups, at bedtime, and during quiet moments.
Sound Therapy and Masking Support
Sound support makes tinnitus less noticeable by giving the brain a healthier sound to focus on.
Your plan can include sound therapy options and masking tools. We show you what these tools do, and we help you use them in the places where tinnitus feels worst.
For many people, sound support is most helpful at night. It also helps during reading, office work, and other quiet activities.
Hearing Aids with Tinnitus Features
If your evaluation shows hearing loss, hearing aids can play an important role.

Modern hearing aids give the brain more outside sound to work with. That reduces the contrast between silence and tinnitus. Many devices also include tinnitus features that add a steady relief sound when you need it.
We select and fit technology based on your hearing needs and your daily life. Your plan stays personal and customized to address your lifestyle.
Daily Habits That Protect Your Progress
Small choices can support tinnitus management.
Hearing protection matters. Loud sound exposure can worsen both hearing loss and tinnitus, so protecting your ears is part of long-term care.
Sleep support matters too. Better rest helps the brain handle stress and ringing in the ears with more resilience.
We also talk about sound balance at home. A little background sound can help in quiet rooms where tinnitus stands out.
Why Choose New Leaf Hearing Care
Tinnitus relief requires trust and follow-through. Our clinic approach supports both.
We listen and help you understand your results and build a plan that fits your goals and your routine.
You also get ongoing support. If your needs change, your plan changes with you.
When to Schedule A Tinnitus Evaluation
Book a tinnitus evaluation if ringing or buzzing is affecting your sleep, focus, or mood.
Many people also benefit from a tinnitus evaluation when the sound feels louder in quiet spaces, or when tinnitus shows up alongside new hearing changes.
Early care helps you feel in control sooner.
Frequently Asked Questions
What is Tinnitus Evaluation and Management?

Tinnitus Evaluation and Management is a clinical service that includes hearing testing, tinnitus-specific questionnaires, and a customized plan that uses counseling and sound tools to reduce tinnitus impact.
How does a tinnitus evaluation work at New Leaf Hearing Care?
Your visit includes a detailed tinnitus history, a full hearing evaluation, additional tinnitus testing and questionnaires, and a demonstration of tinnitus masking options.
How do I get relief from tinnitus during the day?
Relief comes from a plan that reduces stress response and supports sound balance, including counseling and sound therapy tools that lower tinnitus awareness during work, home time, and quiet tasks.
Schedule Your Tinnitus Evaluation
Tinnitus can take up too much space in your day. You deserve a plan that helps you feel steady again.
Schedule with New Leaf Hearing Care in Littleton, CO or Arvada, CO.
We will guide you step by step, review your hearing, and build a customized tinnitus management plan you can use right away.
If you want real support and clear direction, start with Tinnitus Evaluation and Management at New Leaf Hearing Care.

data.meta_description: New Leaf Hearing Care is an authorized provider of Oticon hearing aids in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO. Schedule your fitting today.

children.text: Oticon hearing aids are advanced devices that support clearer speech and more natural sound, using modern processing built to help the brain make sense of what you hear.
Oticon offers multiple styles and tech levels, including options for rechargeability, Bluetooth streaming, tinnitus relief sounds, and solutions for single-sided hearing loss.
New Leaf Hearing Care provides Oticon hearing aid fittings and support in Arvada, CO and Littleton, CO.

Oticon is an industry leader in developing innovative technologies for people with hearing loss. Oticon’s solutions are designed to help you hear and communicate better, so you can enjoy life more fully. Their commitment and dedication to research and development translate to the incredible sound quality of their hearing aids. Oticon believes everyone should have access to the best hearing solutions available today.
Oticon Hearing Aids Available In Colorado
Oticon Zeal
Oticon Zeal is the first hearing aid designed to
do it all

while staying completely unseen. Built for people who want powerful technology in an ultra-discreet design, Zeal delivers exceptional sound clarity, all-day comfort, and seamless connectivity in one tiny device that no one will notice.

Zeal transforms the idea of what a hearing aid should be. Its small, nearly invisible design sits comfortably in your ear, giving you the confidence to live fully without drawing attention to your device. Powered by Oticon’s advanced BrainHearing™ technology, Zeal supports the brain’s natural way of processing sound. Enjoy clear, balanced, and comfortable hearing, even in noisy places.
Unique Features:

Made to Connect: Stream calls, meetings, music, and more directly from your phone, Apple Watch, TV, computer, or tablet. Zeal brings your digital world to life with smooth, high-quality wireless audio.
Reliable Rechargeability: No disposable batteries. With fast, dependable charging, you get all-day power that keeps up with your lifestyle.
Same-Day Fitting: Zeal is designed for convenience, offering the option for same-day fitting so you can begin hearing better right away.
Oticon Intent

Oticon Intent seamlessly adapts to the user’s specific listening needs, even in dynamic sound environments, providing a personalized and immersive auditory experience. This is made possible with the groundbreaking 4D Sensor technology, a pioneering advancement that integrates information from various sources such as head and body movement, conversation activity, and the acoustic environment.

Oticon Intent incorporates the brand new Deep Neural Network, a state-of-the-art neural processing system. This advanced technology employs complex algorithms inspired by the human brain’s neural networks to intelligently analyze and interpret sound inputs. It further enhances the user’s ability to engage with their surroundings and communicate effectively.

Oticon Intent introduces the New MoreSound Amplifier 3.0, a powerful component that expands access to sound. This latest iteration of the MoreSound Amplifier leverages advanced signal processing techniques to amplify and refine incoming sound signals with exceptional clarity and precision. By enhancing the amplification process, Oticon Intent ensures that users can perceive even subtle nuances in their auditory environment, facilitating clearer communication and a richer listening experience.
Unique Features:
Bluetooth® LE Audio: Oticon Intent ensures future-proof connectivity with Bluetooth® LE Audio technology. This allows seamless pairing with various devices for direct audio streaming.

Intelligent miniFit Detect: Oticon Intent introduces the world’s first self-calibrating speaker, providing up to 57% more precise gain adjustment for optimal sound quality tailored to each user’s preferences.
Improved Rechargeability: With more power and 33% shorter charging time, Oticon Intent offers enhanced rechargeability for extended usage throughout the day.
Oticon Jet PX

Oticon Jet PX is a smart hearing aid that uses advanced technology (called Deep Neural Network or DNN) to help you hear clearly. With features like Wind Noise Management and Speech Guard, it helps block out sounds that get in the way of talking to others.

If you have ringing in your ears (tinnitus), Jet PX has built-in sounds to help you feel better. You can also connect it to your phone to listen to music, take calls, or change settings using the Oticon app. It comes in different colors and styles, and you can even get a rechargeable version.
Unique Features:
AI-powered OpenSound Navigator with DNN: Improves hearing with clear, natural sound.
Tinnitus SoundSupport: Ideal for users with tinnitus as it gives access to customizable relief sounds.
SuperShield: Quickly adapts and prevents feedback before it occurs.
Oticon Own SI

Oticon Own SI hearing aids are tiny, comfortable devices that fit inside your ears and help you hear clearly again. It comes with two small styles to choose from – one that’s nearly invisible and another with easy-to-use buttons.
These custom-made hearing aids are built with special BrainHearing technology to support how your brain naturally understands sound, helping you feel more connected to the people around you.
Unique Features:
Discreet and sophisticated: Super small styles (IIC and CIC) that fit fully in your ear
BrainHearing™ technology: Helps your brain make sense of sound.
Custom-fit: Built for all-day comfort, even with glasses or masks.
Strong and durable: Water- and dust-resistant to last through busy days.
Oticon Real

Oticon Real was created to handle the “real” sounds of everyday life. The newest sound processing includes improved technologies that balance sudden disruptive sounds, noise handling (ex: hair or glasses touching hearing aids), and wind noise. The advanced technology of Oticon Real includes a Deep Neural Network (DNN), trained with 12 million real-life sound scenes. Overall, this improved access to sound allows patients to have increased awareness, engagement, and focus in their daily lives.

Oticon Real is currently available in two styles: a mini receiver-in-the-ear (miniRITE) or a mini behind-the-ear (miniBTE). These devices are appropriate for fitting slight-to-profound hearing losses. They offer two battery options: size 312 or rechargeable.
Unique Features:
Bluetooth Connectivity: Streams directly from iPhone and Android™ devices.

App / Remote Help: The Oticon Companion app or Oticon ON app provides support for you to make controls and personalize your fitting independently. Other bonus features inside the app include the “Find my Hearing Aids” feature and Remote Counseling assistance when needed.
Tinnitus Solutions: Oticon offers relief in its Tinnitus SoundSupport™. This feature offers a variety of relief options, including ocean‑like sounds that can be customized for your hearing needs.
Oticon Own

There’s no one-size-fits-all solution when it comes to hearing aids. Oticon Own is crafted to meet your needs in a customized fit to your ear anatomy as well as your lifestyle needs. This product is designed to blend in, so you can stand out first.

Oticon Own is the world’s first in-the-ear (ITE) hearing aid with an onboard Deep Neural Network (DNN). This technology provides you with access to more sound and reduces listening effort. This means that even when you’re in a challenging listening environment — like a restaurant, or a meeting at the office — Oticon Own will help you navigate what you need to hear, and reduce your listening effort, so it’s easier for your brain to take on other tasks.

Oticon Own devices are appropriate for fitting mild-to-severe hearing losses. The custom fit is created based on your ear anatomy and is available in a variety of size options. Depending on the desired hearing aid size, there are several disposable battery options, including size 13, 312, or 10.
Unique Features:
Bluetooth Connectivity: Streams directly from iPhone and Android™ devices.

App / Remote Help: The Oticon Companion app or Oticon ON app provides support for you to independently make controls and personalize your fitting. Other bonus features inside the app include the “Find my Hearing Aids” feature and Remote Counseling assistance when needed.
Tinnitus Solutions: Oticon offers relief in its Tinnitus SoundSupport™. This feature offers a variety of relief options, including ocean‑like sounds that can be customized for your hearing needs.
**Note: Completely-in-the-canal (CIC) and Invisible-in-the-canal (IIC) models do not offer Bluetooth connectivity due to hearing aid size and space limitations.
Oticon Zircon

The Oticon Zircon is an essential line of technology that builds on Oticon’s BrainHearing philosophy. Zircon balances sound from all directions to maintain clear speech, allowing you to comfortably focus on what you want to hear. The OpenSound Navigator helps reduce unwanted noise by scanning your sound environment more than 500 times per second.
This helps to identify unwanted noise and separate it from relevant speech. The end goal is to provide a clear picture of the sound around you, without compromising access to speech.

Oticon Zircon is currently available in two styles: a mini receiver-in-the-ear (miniRITE) or a mini behind-the-ear (miniBTE). These devices are appropriate for fitting mild-to-profound hearing losses. They offer two battery options: size 312 or rechargeable.
Unique Features:
Bluetooth Connectivity: Streams directly from iPhone and Android™ devices.

App / Remote Help: The Oticon Companion app or Oticon ON app provides support for you to independently make controls and personalize your fitting. Other bonus features inside the app include the “Find my Hearing Aids” feature and Remote Counseling assistance when needed.
Tinnitus Solutions: Oticon offers relief in its Tinnitus SoundSupport™. This feature offers a variety of relief options, including ocean‑like sounds that can be customized for your hearing needs.
Oticon CROS

Single-sided deafness can create several listening challenges, including localizing sounds and listening to background noise. The Oticon CROS (contralateral routing of the signal) provides a solution for individuals who are looking for a more fully immersive listening experience. A CROS operates by wearing a transmitter on the unaidable ear, and sounds received from that transmitter are routed to a hearing device worn on the better-hearing ear.

The Oticon CROS is designed for individuals with single-sided deafness and can be fit as either a true CROS or BiCROS (hearing loss is also in the better hearing ear).  There is a choice of a rechargeable Oticon CROS PX or a non-rechargeable Oticon CROS transmitter. Both of these devices are compatible with the following product families: Oticon Real™, More™,  Zircon, Opn S™, Play PX, Opn Play™, Xceed, Xceed Play, and Ruby.
Unique Features:

Bluetooth Connectivity: TwinLink technology makes it possible to connect to external audio sources (music, podcasts, phone calls, etc), while simultaneously receiving CROS sound transmission from the poorer to the better ear. This enables patients to watch television or listen to music while carrying on a conversation with someone positioned on their poorer ear side.

App / Remote Help: The Oticon Companion app or Oticon ON app provides support for you to independently make controls and personalize your fitting. Other bonus features inside the app include the “Find my Hearing Aids” feature and Remote Counseling assistance when needed.
Tinnitus Solutions: Oticon offers relief in its Tinnitus SoundSupport™. This feature offers a variety of relief options, including ocean‑like sounds that can be customized for your hearing needs.
Oticon Play PX

Hearing loss impacts people of all ages. Oticon Play PX brings sound to life by providing children with access to the full sound scene. Play PX is the first pediatric hearing aid with an onboard Deep Neural Network (DNN) that learns the same way children learn – through experience. This advanced technology provides them with optimal hearing for speech and language development, in addition to enjoying the magic of childhood.

Oticon Play PX is currently available in two styles: a mini receiver-in-the-ear (miniRITE) or a mini behind-the-ear (miniBTE). These devices are appropriate for fitting slight-to-severe hearing losses. They offer two battery options: size 312 or rechargeable.
Unique Features:
Tamper-resistant: Styles that help keep the battery out of reach of curious fingers.
Built to last: They carry an IP68 certification, which means they are dust- and water-resistant, able to withstand humidity and varying climates.
Light indicator: A convenient LED light for the power source also gives complete peace of mind, informing caregivers and teachers of visual confirmation that the battery is functioning properly.

Bluetooth connectivity: Streams directly from iPhone and Android™ smartphones. Additionally, Play PX offers hands‑free communication that pairs with select iPhone® and iPad® devices, making direct streaming from children’s favorite devices even easier.

Learning in the classroom can be chaotic enough, and adding hearing loss to the equation can create further difficulty. Oticon offers several unique accessories to provide optimal hearing in challenging listening environments:

EduMic — an easy-to-use classroom hearing solution providing clear and direct access to the teacher’s voice from a wireless remote microphone. This accessory helps children manage noise, reverberation, and distance in the classroom.
Amigo FM — is designed to help students overcome the effects of distance and noise, so they can put less effort into hearing and concentrate on understanding what is being said.

ConnectClip — is a multi-purpose accessory that can transform your child’s hearing aids into high-quality headphones that stream sound to both ears. Even with the best hearing aids, it can sometimes be difficult to clearly hear what someone is saying (ex: on the playground or in the car). The ConnectClip provides a boost in these difficult listening situations by streaming another person’s voice—up to 65 feet away—directly to all Oticon Bluetooth-enabled hearing aids (including Oticon Play PX, Opn Play, or Xceed Play).
Oticon Xceed

Oticon Xceed was designed to give power to those who need it, without creating feedback (whistling) or compromised sound quality. The BrainHearing technology found in Xceed scans your surroundings 100 times per second. In research studies, they found that individuals wearing Xceed hearing aids noticed 10% improvements in speech clarity, 10% less listening effort/strain, and 15% improvements in short-term recall. All of these findings suggest patients have an improved ability to handle noisier environments and improve their listening fatigue.
Oticon Xceed is available in the behind-the-ear (BTE) style. These devices are appropriate for fitting severe-to-profound hearing losses. They offer two battery options: size 13 or 675.
Unique Features:

Bluetooth Connectivity: The phone can be a very difficult listening scenario for individuals with severe-to-profound hearing loss. Oticon Xceed simplifies phone conversations by offering Bluetooth connectivity, which is made for iPhone and connects to any modern smartphone via ConnectClip. This allows for improved, crisp communication over the phone.

App / Remote Help: The Oticon Companion app or Oticon ON app provides support for you to independently make controls and personalize your fitting. Other bonus features inside the app include the “Find my Hearing Aids” feature and Remote Counseling assistance when needed.
Tinnitus Solutions: Oticon offers relief in its Tinnitus SoundSupport™. This feature offers a variety of relief options, including ocean‑like sounds that can be customized for your hearing needs
Oticon Xceed Play
This is the world’s most powerful pediatric hearing aid and the only hearing device on the market that offers an open sound experience for kids with severe to profound hearing loss.

Severe hearing loss impacts people of all ages, including children. Oticon Xceed Play is another pediatric hearing aid designed for severe-to-profound hearing losses. Children with significant hearing loss need more support to separate speech from noise in order to develop speech and language. Xceed Play utilizes BrainHearing™ technology, which gives the child access to clearer speech from all directions for listening and learning. This allows them to learn in a way that mimics the way the brain naturally makes sense of sound.

Oticon Xceed Play is available in the behind-the-ear (BTE) style. These devices are appropriate for fitting pediatric patients with severe-to-profound hearing losses. They offer two battery options: size 13 or 675. Both styles also include a fun collection of decorative stickers your child can use to decorate their hearing aids!
Oticon offers several unique accessories to provide optimal hearing for children in challenging listening environments:

EduMic — an easy-to-use classroom hearing solution providing clear and direct access to the teacher’s voice from a wireless remote microphone. This accessory helps children manage noise, reverberation, and distance in the classroom.
Amigo FM — is designed to help students overcome the effects of distance and noise, so they can put less effort into hearing and concentrate on understanding what is being said.

ConnectClip — is a multi-purpose accessory that can transform your child’s hearing aids into high-quality headphones and stream sounds to both ears. Even with the best hearing aids, it can sometimes be difficult to clearly hear what someone is saying (ex: on the playground or in the car). The ConnectClip provides a boost in these difficult listening situations by streaming another person’s speech—up to 65 feet away—directly to all Oticon Bluetooth-enabled hearing aids (including Oticon Play PX, Opn Play or Xceed Play).

The new Oticon Companion app and Oticon ON app offer a wide variety of features that make life simpler with caring for pediatric hearing aids. The app provides several convenient features like checking the hearing aid battery status, volume adjustments, program changes,  and more. These apps also include a “find my hearing aid” feature if hearing aids are misplaced.
Assistive Listening Devices & Technology by Oticon
Oticon EduMic

EduMic is a classroom hearing solution that provides direct access to the teacher’s voice from a wireless remote microphone to help children manage noise, reverberation, and distance in the classroom. Oticon’s pediatric hearing aids and EduMic work together to maximize children’s speech understanding for language development, learning, and socializing opportunities.

Frequently Asked Questions About Oticon Hearing Aids
What is the life expectancy of an Oticon hearing aid?

Generally speaking, in-the-ear hearing aids tend to last up to five years, while behind-the-ear hearing aids typically last five to six years. Largely, it depends on how well you take care of your hearing aids. Some patients will decide to upgrade their hearing aids prior to this time, as newer technology is routinely released and may provide greater benefits. An audiologist will be able to guide you in deciding when an upgrade is necessary.
Are Oticon hearing aids compatible with Android and iPhone?

Most modern Oticon aids (including the Zeal, Intent, and Real) are "Made for iPhone" (MFi) and support the Android ASHA protocol. Additionally, the 2026 models now feature Bluetooth LE Audio and Auracast™, which provide more stable streaming and better battery efficiency for the latest smartphones.
